blueprint.summary: The data model for this French child protection (ASE) platform centers on Child, Placement, PlacementRequest, Establishment, Report, Document, User, Message, AuditLog, and Notification entities stored in MongoDB with strict multi-tenant isolation via departmentId on all collections. Child records contain GDPR Art. 9 special category health data requiring soft-delete patterns, anonymizable audit trails, and retention until age 25 per French CASF law. Schema versioning will use Prisma migrations or timestamped custom scripts; Excel-based migration tooling will import establishments, users, and historical placements with dry-run validation. Shared-database multi-tenancy chosen for cost and simplicity (1–10 departments, 500–1500 children each), with compound indexes on (departmentId, <field>) for all queries and strict middleware enforcement to prevent cross-tenant leakage. File storage (S3 or Vercel Blob) requires private buckets with presigned URLs and RBAC-controlled access. All collections audited; AuditLog retained 10 years, anonymized on child erasure. Critical indexes ensure tenant isolation, placement matching performance, and audit traceability.

data_model.notes: Contains GDPR Art. 9 special category health data. Soft-delete pattern (deletedAt). Retention: child.dateOfBirth + 25 years (CASF law). Full field list depends on exhaustive placement matching criteria (disability types, behavioral profile, judicial constraints) not yet documented — medium severity doubt.

Discussion threads attached to child dossier. No edit/delete in V1 (audit compliance). GDPR: messages related to erased child are anonymized (content replaced, authorId nulled). Structure depends on whether messaging is chat-like or threaded — UX decision pending (medium doubt).

Anonymizable: userId and ipAddress set to null on child GDPR erasure, but action/timestamp preserved for legal traceability. Retention: 10 years (TTL index on timestamp). GDPR erasure scope unclear (child only vs professional users) — medium severity doubt.

blueprint.migration_strategy: Schema changes managed via Prisma migrations (if Prisma ORM chosen) or custom migration scripts in /migrations folder with timestamps. Migrations applied on deploy via CI/CD pre-deploy hook. MongoDB schema flexibility allows additive changes (new fields) without downtime. Breaking changes (field renames, deletions) require two-phase deploy: (1) add new field + backfill data, (2) deprecate old field in subsequent release. Rollback plan: maintain last known-good schema snapshot in version control. Excel import for pilot: Node.js scripts using xlsx library. Three import stages: (1) Establishment list with capacities, (2) User list (social workers), (3) Historical placements (optional for reporting continuity). Data cleaning layer required (normalize names, validate emails, map inconsistent establishment IDs). Dry-run validation mode before production import. No automated sync — one-time migration only. Excel file quality unknown (likely duplicates, inconsistent naming, missing fields) — cleaning logic will be iterative based on actual pilot data from Département de l'Ain.

retention_policies.reason: French child protection law (CASF Art. L221-1) requires retention until child reaches age 25. Implemented as retentionUntil = dateOfBirth + 25 years (approximately 9125 days from birth). MongoDB TTL index triggers automatic deletion. Related Placement, Report, Document records cascade-deleted or anonymized (strategy not yet finalized — anonymization may be safer for audit purposes). GDPR Art. 9 special category health data. Exact retention duration not confirmed by client DPO — could be longer for certain judicial cases.

Document retention follows Child retention policy (age 25). Metadata stored in MongoDB with TTL index on retentionUntil. Physical files in S3/Vercel Blob must be deleted via lifecycle policy synchronized with MongoDB TTL. GDPR deletion requires both metadata removal and physical file deletion. Cloud storage provider not yet selected — S3 lifecycle policies well-supported, Vercel Blob retention automation unclear.

Audit logs retained 10 years for legal traceability (standard French public record retention). TTL index on AuditLog.timestamp triggers automatic deletion after 10 years. Anonymization applied after Child erasure (userId and ipAddress nulled), but log structure preserved for compliance. 10-year retention not confirmed by client legal team — may need alignment with French public archives regulation.

Placements logically tied to Child records. Retention strategy follows Child retention (cascade-deleted or anonymized when child record reaches TTL). No independent TTL — lifecycle managed via Child deletion workflow.
Reports tied to Child and Placement. Retention follows Child retention (cascade-deleted or anonymized). No independent TTL.

Messages tied to Child dossier. Retention follows Child retention. GDPR erasure: messages related to erased child are anonymized (content replaced with '[ANONYMIZED]', authorId nulled). No independent TTL.

In-app notifications retained 1 year for user reference, then automatically deleted. Not tied to Child retention (notifications are operational data, not child protection records). TTL index on Notification.createdAt.

User accounts retained indefinitely (no automatic deletion). Inactive users may be manually archived by department admin. GDPR right to erasure applies if user requests deletion (separate from child data erasure).

Establishment records retained indefinitely (no automatic deletion). Establishments may be marked inactive if no longer in service, but historical placement data requires persistent establishment references.

risks.description: Shared MongoDB database with departmentId filtering creates blocking risk of accidental cross-tenant data leakage if backend query logic omits departmentId filter. Given GDPR Art. 9 health data and multi-department architecture, a single query bug could expose child records across departments, violating GDPR Art. 5 (data minimization) and Art. 32 (security). Reputational and legal risk for public institution.
risks.mitigation: (1) Middleware enforces departmentId injection on all database queries (no query allowed without departmentId filter). (2) Automated integration tests verify tenant isolation (e.g., attempt to query departmentId=A while authenticated as departmentId=B, must fail). (3) MongoDB pre-query hooks (if using Mongoose ORM) enforce departmentId presence at schema level. (4) Code review checklist includes tenant isolation verification. (5) Periodic security audits of query logic.

risks.description: Documents stored in S3/Vercel Blob must not be accessible via direct public URLs, as they contain GDPR Art. 9 health data (medical documents, judicial records). If cloud storage bucket is misconfigured as public or presigned URLs are not properly scoped, unauthorized access becomes possible. GDPR Art. 32 violation.
risks.mitigation: (1) S3 bucket configured as private (no public access). (2) Presigned URLs generated on-demand by backend API after RBAC validation (5-minute expiry, single-use preferred). (3) AuditLog records all document downloads (userId, timestamp, documentId). (4) Backend validates user permission before generating presigned URL. (5) Vercel Blob access control model not yet validated — may not support presigned URLs, requiring proxy download through API route (adds latency but ensures RBAC enforcement).

risks.area: Exact retention duration under French CASF law
risks.description: Data retention policy assumes 25-year retention for child records (child reaches age 25) based on CASF Art. L221-1 and related regulations. However, exact retention duration not confirmed by client DPO. Some judicial cases may require longer retention. If TTL is set incorrectly, premature deletion could violate legal archival requirements or delayed deletion could violate GDPR data minimization.
risks.mitigation: (1) Validate retention duration with client DPO and legal team before production launch. (2) Implement retentionUntil as calculated field (dateOfBirth + retention years), allowing adjustment if legal requirement changes. (3) Manual override capability for department admin to extend retention on specific child records (e.g., ongoing judicial cases). (4) Audit trail preserved even after child record deletion (anonymized AuditLog retained 10 years).

risks.description: When Child record reaches retention TTL (age 25), related records (Placement, Report, Document, Message) must be cascade-deleted or anonymized. Strategy not yet finalized. Cascade deletion is simpler but may violate audit traceability requirements (e.g., historical placement statistics, establishment performance reports). Anonymization preserves structure but requires careful field-by-field redaction logic.
risks.mitigation: (1) Recommend anonymization over deletion for Placement, Report (preserve statistical data, anonymize personal identifiers). (2) Document metadata anonymized (fileName, uploader), physical files deleted. (3) Messages anonymized (content replaced with '[ANONYMIZED]'). (4) Audit trail preserved (AuditLog anonymizes userId but retains action/timestamp). (5) Finalize strategy with legal team and implement before pilot launch.

risks.description: Excel files from Département de l'Ain likely contain duplicates, inconsistent establishment naming, missing fields, outdated records. Poor import data quality will undermine pilot success (incorrect establishment capacities, missing user accounts, broken placement history references). Data cleaning is labor-intensive and may delay pilot timeline.
risks.mitigation: (1) Dry-run validation script generates error report before production import (duplicate detection, schema validation, referential integrity checks). (2) Manual data cleaning phase with client (normalize establishment names, validate emails, deduplicate records). (3) Fallback: start with empty database and manual data entry if import fails (slower but guarantees data quality). (4) Iterative import process — import establishments first, validate, then users, then placements. (5) Import tooling logs all skipped/failed rows for manual review.

risks.area: Cloud storage provider not yet selected
risks.description: PM tensions suggest S3 or Vercel Blob for document storage, but provider not yet decided. S3 is not part of the fixed Next.js/Vercel stack specified by CTO. Vercel Blob is native to Vercel but has unclear support for presigned URLs, lifecycle policies, and large medical documents (50MB+ PDFs). Choice affects access control strategy, retention automation, and cost.
risks.mitigation: (1) Clarify with CTO whether S3 integration is acceptable or Vercel Blob is mandated. (2) If Vercel Blob: validate presigned URL support and lifecycle policy capabilities before schema finalization. If unsupported, fallback to proxy download through API route (RBAC enforced, but adds latency). (3) If S3: confirm AWS SDK integration is acceptable within Vercel serverless environment (credentials in environment variables, rotation policy).

risks.description: Backend lead has not yet decided ORM (Prisma vs Mongoose vs native MongoDB driver). Choice affects schema migration strategy, query enforcement (e.g., Mongoose pre-query hooks for departmentId injection), and TTL index management. Prisma's MongoDB support is production-ready but less mature than PostgreSQL support. Mongoose offers richer MongoDB-specific features (middleware, virtuals) but requires more manual migration scripting.
risks.mitigation: (1) Backend lead decides ORM within 1 week of data architecture delivery. (2) If Prisma: use Prisma Migrate for schema versioning (automatic migration generation, rollback support). (3) If Mongoose: custom migration scripts in /migrations folder with timestamps, applied via CI/CD pre-deploy hook. (4) If native driver: manual schema evolution, higher risk of inconsistent schema across environments (not recommended given GDPR compliance requirements).

risks.area: Full placement matching criteria not yet exhaustive
risks.description: Placement matching logic depends on child profile vs establishment criteria (age, gender, special needs confirmed). However, PM tensions note additional criteria mentioned but not detailed: disability types, behavioral profile, geographic proximity, judicial order constraints. If matching criteria schema is incomplete, placement matching UX will fail to surface relevant establishments, defeating core value proposition (faster placement search from 1–3 days to same day).
risks.mitigation: (1) UX Architect and PM work with client (Département de l'Ain) to exhaustively enumerate matching criteria during design phase. (2) Schema designed with extensibility: Establishment.matchingCriteria as flexible JSON object, allowing new criteria to be added without schema migration. (3) Compound index on Establishment designed to support common query patterns; additional indexes added as criteria are defined. (4) V1 launches with known criteria (age, gender, capacity, special needs); additional criteria added in V1.1 based on pilot feedback.

blockers.decision: Finalize RBAC permission matrix (roles × collections × actions)
blockers.blocked_by: pm_users_roles_external and back_rbac_permission_matrix — exact data access perimeter per external role type not yet defined (magistrates read judicial docs only, doctors read/write health data only, teachers read schooling data only). Backend lead flagged this as blocking for permission middleware implementation. Data Architect cannot finalize Document.category enum or Child schema field groupings (e.g., medicalData, judicialData, schoolingData sub-objects) until permission boundaries are defined.

blockers.decision: Finalize full Child entity field list for placement matching
blockers.blocked_by: pm_business_rules_placement_matching — full matching criteria (disability types, behavioral profile, geographic proximity, judicial constraints) not yet exhaustive. Child schema needs these fields for matching logic, and Establishment schema needs corresponding criteria fields. Medium severity doubt in data_schema_design_core and data_index_strategy_matching.

blueprint.summary: Building a GDPR-compliant multi-tenant child protection platform for French ASE departments. Backend uses Next.js API Routes on Vercel with MongoDB/Prisma. Authentication via NextAuth.js with mandatory MFA for health data access (TOTP). REST API with versioned routes (/api/v1/*), cursor-based pagination, and strict multi-tenant scoping enforced via middleware. RBAC with closed-by-default permissions, full audit trail with anonymization capability for GDPR Art. 9 compliance. Key integrations: AWS S3 for document storage with pre-signed URLs, SendGrid for transactional email, @react-pdf/renderer for server-side PDF generation. Real-time dashboard via polling (30s interval) due to Vercel serverless constraints. Placement matching algorithm filters establishments by child profile (age, gender, needs). All write operations logged to anonymizable AuditLog collection.

auth_design.flow: 1. Admin creates invite via POST /api/v1/auth/invite → invite link generated with 48h expiry. 2. User registers via POST /api/v1/auth/register with inviteToken + password (min 12 chars, complex). 3. User receives JWT (8h expiry) + refresh token. 4. MFA (TOTP) required for first login if user has health data access. 5. Refresh token rotation on each use. 6. Middleware validates JWT + enforces departmentId scoping + RBAC permissions (role + groups) on every API route.
auth_design.notes: MFA assumed mandatory for health data access per GDPR Art. 9 but needs client confirmation (pm_security_auth lists as 'strongly recommended'). Permission matrix blocks finalization of scoped endpoints until external role access perimeters are legally validated (magistrates, doctors, teachers). Closed-by-default RBAC: all permissions denied unless explicitly granted via role or group assignment.

integrations.purpose: Document storage for medical/judicial/administrative child documents
integrations.approach: Server-side SDK (@aws-sdk/client-s3) in Next.js API routes. Pre-signed URLs for upload (15min) and download (5min, single-use). Metadata in MongoDB, files in S3 with bucket structure {departmentId}/{childId}/{documentId}. GDPR deletion = S3 object delete + MongoDB soft-delete.

integrations.purpose: Transactional email (placement notifications, report alerts, user invites, password resets)
integrations.approach: Server-side SDK via Next.js API route /api/internal/send-email. Templates stored in SendGrid. Retry logic (3 attempts, exponential backoff). Bounce/complaint webhooks logged to MongoDB EmailLog collection. No sensitive child data in email body.

integrations.purpose: Server-side PDF generation for reports and documents
integrations.approach: React-based PDF templates rendered in Next.js API route POST /api/v1/documents/generate. Templates: judicial_report, medical_summary, placement_history, activity_report. Generated PDF uploaded to S3, download URL returned. Typed data props per template.
upstash-ratelimit library integrated into Next.js API middleware. Alternative: Vercel Edge Config if Redis not approved. Per-user rate limit enforced before RBAC check.

risks.description: External professional role permissions (doctors, magistrates, teachers) not yet legally validated. Blocking finalization of scoped document/child access endpoints. Risk: incorrect access scope violates GDPR Art. 9 or French health data regulations.
risks.mitigation: Escalate to client legal/DPO for role access validation. Cannot deploy scoped endpoints until resolved. Temporary: disable external professional access in V1 pilot, restrict to department workers + establishments only.

risks.description: MongoDB + Prisma multi-tenant architecture via departmentId scoping must be fail-safe. Single middleware bug could leak child health data across departments (catastrophic GDPR breach).
risks.mitigation: Mandatory: (1) Prisma middleware enforces departmentId filter on all queries, (2) integration tests verify cross-tenant isolation, (3) code review by data architect before any deployment, (4) recommend separate MongoDB databases per tenant (higher cost, zero leak risk).

risks.description: Polling-based availability dashboard (30s interval) may not meet user expectation of 'real-time'. If <5s latency required, Vercel serverless architecture cannot deliver without external service (Pusher, Ably) — outside fixed stack.
risks.mitigation: UX Architect to validate 30s polling is acceptable. If not, flag as stack blocker requiring client approval for external WebSocket service or migration to long-running server architecture.

risks.description: Prisma's MongoDB connector lacks transactions across collections, full-text search, and advanced aggregation. GDPR audit trail and placement matching may hit performance/feature limits at scale.
risks.mitigation: Recommend PostgreSQL substitution (better Prisma support, ACID transactions). If MongoDB is mandatory, architect workarounds (e.g., denormalization for audit trail, client-side search filtering). Flag to client as technical debt.

risks.description: Stack specifies Vercel but large medical documents likely need S3 or equivalent. S3 not part of fixed stack. Vercel Blob has file size/cost limits unsuitable for long-term medical document retention.
risks.mitigation: Confirm with client: (1) S3 integration acceptable, or (2) Vercel Blob sufficient (requires file size limit validation), or (3) alternative storage provider mandated. Blocker until resolved.

risks.description: PM tension pm_security_auth lists MFA as 'strongly recommended' but not confirmed by client. GDPR Art. 9 health data strongly implies MFA should be mandatory. Risk: client rejects MFA requirement post-development, forcing architecture change.
risks.mitigation: Escalate to client for explicit MFA mandate confirmation. Design MFA as optional flag per user role (can be toggled mandatory later) to minimize rework if requirement changes.

risks.description: PDF generation endpoints depend on template definitions (judicial_report, medical_summary, etc.) but data sources and layouts not yet specified (pm_technical_document_generation medium doubt). Cannot implement without schemas.
risks.mitigation: Data Architect + UX Architect must define template data schemas and layouts. Block PDF generation implementation until resolved. Placeholder: generate generic JSON export as interim.

risks.description: External professionals (family assistants, some establishment workers) have varying digital literacy (pm_constraints_adoption_risk). Complex API-driven workflows may fail if UX is not simplified. Backend API must support simplified flows.
risks.mitigation: UX Architect to design progressive disclosure UI. Backend provides flexible endpoints (e.g., simplified POST /api/v1/placements/quick for low-literacy users). Onboarding/training plan needed (outside backend scope).

risks.description: Excel import from legacy systems likely has inconsistent/dirty data (pm_data_import medium doubt). Import tooling may fail or create corrupted records if data quality is poor.
risks.mitigation: Request sample Excel files from client before finalizing import schema. Implement robust validation + error reporting in POST /api/v1/admin/import. Dry-run mode mandatory before production import. Budget cleanup time.

blockers.decision: Finalize RBAC permission matrix and scoped API endpoints (GET /api/v1/children/:id with role-based field filtering, document download permissions per type)
blockers.blocked_by: pm_users_roles_external (external professional role access perimeters not legally validated) — back_rbac_permission_matrix marked as blocking doubt. Cannot implement scoped endpoints without knowing which roles access which document types and child data fields.

blockers.decision: Confirm file storage provider (AWS S3 vs Vercel Blob vs other)
blockers.blocked_by: pm_technical_file_storage (provider not yet decided) — back_integration_file_storage marked as medium doubt. S3 not part of fixed Next.js/Vercel stack. Need client confirmation before implementing document upload/download endpoints.

blockers.decision: Finalize placement matching criteria and algorithm
blockers.blocked_by: pm_business_rules_placement_matching (full matching criteria not exhaustive: disability, behavioral profile, geographic proximity, judicial constraints) — back_placement_matching_logic marked as medium doubt. Cannot finalize POST /api/v1/placements/match logic.

blockers.decision: Finalize PDF document templates and data schemas
blockers.blocked_by: pm_technical_document_generation (template list and data sources not specified) — back_integration_pdf_generation marked as medium doubt. Cannot implement POST /api/v1/documents/generate without template definitions.

blockers.decision: Confirm MongoDB vs PostgreSQL for production
blockers.blocked_by: back_challenge_mongodb_prisma (MongoDB + Prisma has limitations for GDPR audit and multi-tenant isolation) — no upstream blocker but stack decision needs client approval. Recommend PostgreSQL for ACID compliance and better Prisma support.

blueprint.summary: Building a multi-tenant child protection platform (ASE France) for departmental social workers, establishments, and external professionals. Core features: real-time available placement dashboard, child dossier management with GDPR-compliant audit trail, placement request workflow with matching, document management, messaging, and role-based access control. Next.js App Router with role-based route groups, NextAuth.js for authentication, TanStack Query for server state, Tailwind + shadcn/ui for components. Server Components by default, Client Components for interactivity. RBAC enforced at middleware and component level. Multi-tenant isolation via departmentId scoping in session. No SSO/Active Directory — email/password + invite-based registration. Adoption risk mitigated via accessible, simple UX for low digital literacy users.

components.responsibility: Child dossier Overview tab: demographic info, current placement, assigned caseworker, key dates.

components.name: MedicalTab
components.responsibility: Child dossier Medical tab: health data, treatments, disabilities, psychological follow-up. RBAC-restricted to authorized roles only.

components.name: JudicialTab
components.responsibility: Child dossier Judicial tab: court orders, legal status, judicial documents. RBAC-restricted.

components.name: SchoolingTab
components.responsibility: Child dossier Schooling tab: school records, academic tracking. May be simple text fields or structured forms.

components.name: ProfessionalTab
components.responsibility: Child dossier Professional tab: vocational training, employment tracking.

components.name: DocumentsTab
components.responsibility: Child dossier Documents tab: uploaded files grouped by category (judicial, medical, administrative). Upload, view, download, delete. Shows access log.

components.name: ReportsTab
components.responsibility: Child dossier Reports tab: activity reports filed by establishments, chronological list, filterable by date/author.

components.name: MessagesTab
components.responsibility: Child dossier Messages tab: discussion thread for professionals. Chronological list, composer at bottom. No real-time updates in V1 (manual refresh).

components.name: HistoryTab
components.responsibility: Child dossier History tab: audit log of all changes to dossier. Anonymizable post-GDPR erasure.

components.name: CreatePlacementRequestWizard
components.responsibility: 3-step wizard component for placement request creation. Manages form state across steps, validates on each step, submits on final step.

components.name: MatchedEstablishmentsList
components.responsibility: Shows filtered/matched establishments for placement request. May show compatibility score if backend provides it. Checkbox selection for multi-target requests.

components.name: PlacementRequestsTable
components.responsibility: Data table for placement requests list. Filterable by status, sortable by date. Row click navigates to detail.

components.name: PlacementRequestDetail
components.responsibility: Detail view component showing placement request info, child profile, targeted establishments, status per establishment, response messages.

components.name: AvailablePlacesDashboard
components.responsibility: Real-time table of available placement spots. Filters, sort, color-coded availability. Polls every 30s or uses SSE if implemented.

components.name: EstablishmentsTable
components.responsibility: Data table for establishments directory. Filter by type, search by name, row click navigates to profile.

components.name: UsersTable
components.responsibility: Data table for admin user management. Shows role badges, status, last login. Actions: invite, edit, deactivate.

components.name: InviteUserModal
components.responsibility: Modal for admin to invite new user. Fields: email, role. Generates invite link, shows copy button or triggers email send.

components.name: EditUserModal
components.responsibility: Modal for admin to edit user role and permissions. Role dropdown, permissions matrix checkboxes (pending backend /api/roles endpoint).

components.name: AuditLogTable
components.responsibility: Data table for audit log viewer. Filterable by user, action, resource, date. Shows anonymized entries post-GDPR erasure.

components.name: IncomingRequestsList
components.responsibility: Dashboard widget for establishment showing incoming placement requests with urgency badges.

components.name: CapacityWidget
components.responsibility: Dashboard widget showing establishment current capacity (total, occupied, available). Click to update.

components.name: RequestsTable
components.responsibility: Data table for establishment requests list. Filterable by status, sortable by date.

components.name: RequestDetail
components.responsibility: Detail view component for establishment viewing placement request. Shows scoped child profile.

components.name: AcceptRejectForm
components.responsibility: Form for establishment to accept/reject placement request with optional message.

components.name: ReportsTable
components.responsibility: Data table for establishment reports list. Click to view/edit report.

components.name: CreateReportModal
components.responsibility: Modal for establishment to create new activity report for a child.

components.name: CapacityForm
components.responsibility: Form for establishment to update available capacity. Fields: total spots, occupied, available (auto-calculated).

components.name: AssignedChildrenTable
components.responsibility: Data table for external professional showing only assigned children. Scoped by backend RBAC.

components.name: ScopedChildDossier
components.responsibility: Child dossier component for external professional. Only renders tabs user has permission to access.

components.name: DocumentUpload
components.responsibility: File upload component using react-dropzone. Client-side validation (type, size). Shows progress indicator, thumbnail preview.

components.name: DocumentViewer
components.responsibility: Inline document viewer (PDF via react-pdf, images via img tag). Download button. Triggers audit log entry on view/download.

components.name: MessageThread
components.responsibility: Chronological message list with composer. Optimistic updates. No real-time refresh in V1. Shows author role badge.

components.name: AuthMiddleware
components.responsibility: Next.js middleware at /middleware.ts. Validates NextAuth session, checks user role, enforces route access, redirects unauthorized to /auth/login. Multi-tenant isolation via session.user.departmentId.

components.name: RBACProvider
components.responsibility: React Context provider for usePermissions hook. Wraps session permissions array, exposes hasPermission(resource, action) helper.

components.name: ReactQueryProvider
components.responsibility: TanStack Query provider wrapping app. Configures cache, retry, and refetch policies.

ux_flows.name: Department Worker - Create Placement Request
steps: Step 1: Select child from dropdown (filtered by departmentId)
Step 2: Define placement criteria (age range, gender, special needs, urgency)
Step 3: View matched establishments (filtered by criteria), select one or multiple via checkboxes
ux_flows.notes: Multi-establishment request supported. Matching algorithm backend-dependent. UX assumes binary match (yes/no), may show compatibility score if backend provides it.

ux_flows.name: Establishment User - Respond to Placement Request
steps: Receive notification of new placement request
Review scoped child profile (age, gender, needs; medical data may be restricted)
Accept or reject with optional message
ux_flows.notes: Establishment sees scoped child profile per RBAC rules. Acceptance workflow unclear (can they propose alternative dates?). Assumed accept/reject only for V1.

ux_flows.name: Department Worker - Manage Child Dossier
steps: Navigate between tabs (Overview, Medical, Documents, Reports, Messages, etc.)
Upload documents, file reports, send messages, review history
Changes auto-saved or explicitly submitted per form
ux_flows.notes: Tabbed interface with progressive disclosure. RBAC hides tabs for external professionals. Medical tab RBAC-restricted.

ux_flows.name: Department Admin - Invite External Professional
steps: Copy link or trigger email send (if backend supports)
External professional receives invite email, clicks link, sets password, logs in
ux_flows.notes: Invite link expires after 48h. External professional authentication flow not fully specified. MFA may be required.

ux_flows.name: External Professional - Access Assigned Child Dossier
steps: Receive invite email with link to /auth/invite?token=...
Click link, validate token, set password
See list of assigned children only (RBAC-scoped)
Click child to view scoped dossier (/external/children/[childId])
See only permitted tabs (e.g., psychologist sees Medical + Messages, teacher sees Schooling)
ux_flows.notes: Scoped access per role type. Permission matrix not yet fully defined. External professional cannot access placement request workflow or available places dashboard.

blueprint.state_management: React Server Components by default. NextAuth session via useSession hook for auth state. TanStack Query (React Query) for server state caching and optimistic updates on mutations (child dossier edits, placement request submission, report filing). Zustand for ephemeral UI state only (modal open/close, sidebar expanded, dashboard filters). Forms managed via React Hook Form + Zod validation schemas shared with backend. No global Redux.

risks.description: Frontend development blocked on several backend API endpoint contracts (notification list schema, placement match response, child dossier data structure, document upload flow, roles/permissions matrix). Components like NotificationBell, MatchedEstablishmentsList, ChildDossierPage, DocumentViewer, EditUserModal cannot be implemented beyond mockups.
risks.mitigation: Backend architect prioritizes API contract definition. Use OpenAPI/Swagger spec or TypeScript shared types. Frontend mocks endpoints with MSW for parallel development.

risks.description: Real-time dashboard and notification delivery mechanism not finalized (polling vs SSE). PM specifies 'real-time' but Vercel serverless constraints make WebSocket impractical. 30s polling may not meet user expectations for urgent placement requests.
risks.mitigation: Validate 30s polling interval with UX architect and pilot users. If insufficient, adopt SSE via Next.js API route or external service (Pusher, Ably). Clarify stack constraints.

risks.description: Exact data access perimeter per external role type (magistrate, doctor, teacher, psychologist, family assistant) not yet defined. Child dossier tab visibility, message thread access, document access depend on this. Front-end cannot finalize ScopedChildDossier, MessageThread, DocumentsTab without backend permission matrix.
risks.mitigation: PM and backend architect define granular permission matrix (roles × resources × actions). UX architect validates with user research. Front-end implements usePermissions hook once matrix finalized.

risks.description: File storage backend (Vercel Blob vs S3) not decided. Affects DocumentViewer download URL generation (presigned URLs for S3 vs different API for Vercel Blob). DocumentUpload and DocumentViewer components partially speculative.
risks.mitigation: Backend architect chooses storage provider early. If S3, confirm AWS SDK integration is acceptable outside fixed stack. If Vercel Blob, validate access control capabilities.

risks.description: MFA flagged as 'strongly recommended' by PM but not confirmed as mandatory. If required, affects /auth/mfa-setup and /auth/mfa-verify flows, session payload structure, middleware enforcement. MFASetupPage is speculative.
risks.mitigation: Confirm MFA requirement with client given GDPR Art. 9 health data sensitivity. If mandatory, prioritize MFA flow in V1. If deferred, remove MFASetupPage from component tree.

risks.description: Placement matching filter criteria (age, gender, needs) confirmed, but additional criteria (disability types, behavioral profile, geographic proximity, judicial constraints) mentioned but not exhaustively defined. MatchedEstablishmentsList UI depends on complete filter list and backend matching algorithm output (binary match vs ranked compatibility score).
risks.mitigation: PM and backend architect finalize matching criteria list. UX architect designs filter UI. Front-end builds extensible filter architecture to accommodate future criteria.

risks.description: Establishment staff (especially family assistants) and external professionals may have low digital literacy. Complex UI or poor onboarding could derail pilot success with Ain department. No change management plan yet defined by client.
risks.mitigation: UX architect conducts user research with pilot department. Design simple, guided onboarding flow with in-app tooltips. Provide training materials (videos, guides). Plan support channel (email/phone). Iterate UX based on pilot feedback.

risks.description: Shared MongoDB database with departmentId filtering creates risk of cross-tenant data exposure if backend query logic omits departmentId. Front-end relies on backend enforcement via middleware and session scoping.
risks.mitigation: Backend architect implements fail-safe middleware with automated integration tests for tenant isolation. Front-end always passes session.user.departmentId in API calls but cannot independently enforce isolation. Requires backend diligence.

blockers.decision: ScopedChildDossier tab visibility logic and MessageThread access rules for external professionals
blockers.blocked_by: pm_users_roles_external — exact data access perimeter per external role type not yet defined
pm_security_rbac and backend /api/roles endpoint design — granular permission matrix not yet provided

blockers.decision: MatchedEstablishmentsList ranking display and filter criteria UI
blockers.blocked_by: pm_business_rules_placement_matching — full matching criteria not yet exhaustive, backend matching algorithm output schema unknown
pm_technical_file_storage — cloud storage provider (S3 vs Vercel Blob) not yet decided
pm_technical_realtime — real-time delivery mechanism (polling vs SSE) not finalized

blockers.decision: MFASetupPage implementation and enforcement in AuthMiddleware
blockers.blocked_by: pm_security_auth — MFA requirement not confirmed as mandatory for V1

blueprint.summary: UX architecture for a GDPR-compliant child protection placement platform for French ASE departments. The platform serves four primary user types: department social workers managing child dossiers and placement requests, establishment users receiving requests and filing reports, administrators managing roles and permissions, and external professionals (doctors, magistrates, teachers, psychologists) with scoped read/write access. Key design decisions: desktop-first responsive strategy (social workers at desk) with mobile-optimized flows for establishments and external professionals; WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility to ensure usability for diverse digital literacy levels; closed-by-default RBAC with granular permission matrix for external professionals; tabbed child dossier interface with progressive disclosure; multi-establishment placement request workflow with aggregated status tracking; persistent, manually-dismissible notifications for WCAG compliance. Critical UX challenges include designing the permission matrix UI for complex role-based data access and ensuring adoption with users transitioning from Excel-based workflows.

user_journeys.description: Manages child dossiers and placement requests, aiming to find appropriate placements faster (currently 1-3 days, target same day)
key_steps: Create or access existing child dossier with judicial, medical, schooling, and administrative data
View available places dashboard with filters for age, gender, special needs, location, establishment type
Create placement request targeting one or multiple establishments simultaneously
Track request status across multiple establishments (pending/accepted/declined)
Receive notifications when establishments respond or file reports
Complete child dossier by uploading documents (judicial, medical, administrative)
Review activity reports and compte-rendus filed by establishments
Communicate with other professionals via child-centered messaging thread
entry_points: Dashboard showing active placement requests and available places
Notification of new report or message
critical_moments: Multi-establishment placement request form — must clearly show filtered establishments and selection
Placement request status view — must surface decline reasons and next actions if all establishments decline
Document upload with GDPR audit trail — must be reliable, show who accessed/downloaded
Permission-denied errors when external professional attempts unauthorized access

user_journeys.description: Receives placement requests, manages available places, files reports on placed children
key_steps: Receive notification of placement request
View placement request with child profile summary (age, gender, needs)
Review scoped child dossier sections (medical data may be restricted per GDPR minimization)
Accept or decline request with optional note/reason
Update establishment profile to signal available or unavailable places
File activity reports and compte-rendus for placed children
Upload documents related to child follow-up (schooling, therapy progress)
Receive notifications from social workers or other professionals
critical_moments: Placement request decision — must have sufficient child information to decide, but not over-expose sensitive health data
Available place update — workflow for signaling capacity changes must be simple and fast
Report submission — must trigger notification to caseworker, confirm successful upload

user_journeys.description: Manages user accounts, roles, access rights, and department settings within closed-by-default RBAC architecture
key_steps: Invite new users (social workers, external professionals) via email with 48h expiry token
Assign roles and groups per closed-by-default RBAC
Grant or revoke granular permissions for external professionals (e.g., magistrate sees judicial docs only, doctor sees health data only)
View audit log for GDPR compliance review (all create/update/delete/access events)
Manage establishment accounts (create, suspend, edit)
Configure department-level settings (notification preferences, document templates)
critical_moments: Permission matrix UI — complex multi-dimensional assignment (role × data section × action), must be clear and error-resistant
Audit log review — must support filtering by user, date, action type, resource to identify compliance issues
User invitation — must handle expired tokens, re-invitation, and MFA setup

user_journeys.role: External Professional (Psychologist, Doctor, Teacher, Magistrate, Family Assistant)
user_journeys.description: Invited by department admin to access specific child dossier sections within scoped permissions, uploads documents/reports within permitted scope
key_steps: Receive invitation email with scoped access link (may be single-use or time-limited)
Authenticate with MFA (required given GDPR Art. 9 health data)
View assigned child dossier sections only (magistrate: judicial docs; doctor: health data; teacher: schooling)
Upload documents or reports within permitted scope
Read or write in child-centered messaging thread (if granted permission)
No access to placement request workflow, available places dashboard, or other children
critical_moments: Permission-denied errors — must be clear, empathetic, provide admin contact info
MFA setup on first login — must be simple for users with varying digital literacy
Scoped dossier view — must hide inaccessible sections without confusion (not show locked tabs, only show permitted tabs)

blueprint.information_architecture: Top-level navigation structure: (1) **Tableau de bord (Dashboard)** — real-time available places, active placement requests, recent notifications, quick stats; (2) **Dossiers enfants (Child Dossiers)** — searchable/filterable list view, detail view with tabs: *Info générale* (demographics, profile), *Documents* (expandable by category: judicial, medical, administrative), *Historique des placements* (timeline), *Rapports* (filterable by date/author), *Suivi scolaire/professionnel* (schooling and vocational tracking, accordion sections), *Messagerie* (threaded professional communication); (3) **Demandes de placement (Placement Requests)** — create multi-target request, track status per establishment, view decline reasons; (4) **Établissements (Establishments)** — directory, capacity overview, profiles with criteria/special needs support; (5) **Messagerie (Messaging)** — child-centered threads, recent conversations; (6) **Administration (Admin-only)** — user management, role/permission assignment, audit log, department settings. **Mobile**: bottom tab navigation (Dashboard, Dossiers, Demandes, Messagerie, Profile). **Desktop**: left sidebar with collapsible sections, multi-column dashboard layout. **External professionals** see reduced navigation (only Dossiers they have access to, Messagerie if permitted; no Dashboard, Demandes, Établissements, Administration). Tab visibility in child dossier is role-dependent (conditionally shown based on permission matrix).

accessibility_requirements.requirement: Keyboard navigation for all interactions: forms, filters, document upload, placement request multi-select, tab navigation, modal dialogs
accessibility_requirements.rationale: Users with motor disabilities or those preferring keyboard-only interaction must be able to complete all workflows without mouse.

accessibility_requirements.requirement: Screen reader support: semantic HTML5, ARIA labels for dynamic content (real-time dashboard updates, toast notifications, status changes), ARIA live regions for notifications
accessibility_requirements.rationale: Visually impaired social workers, educators, or external professionals must be able to navigate child dossiers, read reports, and receive notifications via screen reader.

accessibility_requirements.requirement: Color contrast ratio ≥4.5:1 for body text, ≥3:1 for UI components and icons, high-contrast mode support
accessibility_requirements.rationale: Ensures readability for users with low vision or color blindness, especially critical for status indicators (placement accepted/declined).

accessibility_requirements.requirement: Focus indicators visible on all interactive elements (buttons, links, form inputs, checkboxes, tabs)
accessibility_requirements.rationale: Keyboard users must see where focus is at all times to navigate efficiently.

accessibility_requirements.requirement: Form validation messages announced to screen readers, focus moved to first error field on submit attempt
accessibility_requirements.rationale: Users relying on assistive technology must receive immediate feedback on validation errors.

accessibility_requirements.requirement: PDF documents uploaded must be accessible (tagged PDFs) where possible; platform-generated PDFs must be tagged
accessibility_requirements.rationale: Judicial, medical, and administrative documents must be readable by screen readers.

accessibility_requirements.requirement: Time-based notifications must not auto-dismiss; users dismiss manually or navigate away
accessibility_requirements.rationale: Users with cognitive or reading disabilities need time to read and act on notifications without time pressure.

accessibility_requirements.requirement: Alternative text for all document icons, status indicators (placement pending/accepted/declined), and illustrations in empty states
accessibility_requirements.rationale: Screen reader users must understand UI state and context through descriptive alt text.

responsive_strategy.rationale: Department social workers (primary users) spend most of their time at desks managing child dossiers, reviewing reports, and processing placement requests. Desktop provides richer data tables, multi-column layouts, and more efficient navigation for complex workflows (permission assignment, audit log review, multi-document upload). However, mobile is critical for establishments (MECS, foyers, family assistants) and external professionals (psychologists, teachers on the move) who need to respond to placement requests, file reports, and upload documents in the field.
responsive_strategy.breakpoint_notes: Breakpoints: mobile (<768px), tablet (768–1024px), desktop (≥1024px). **Mobile**: single-column layout, bottom tab navigation (Dashboard, Dossiers, Demandes, Messagerie, Profile), simplified dashboard (cards instead of tables), touch-optimized inputs (large tap targets for file upload, date pickers, checkboxes), collapsible filters, inline document list (no multi-column table). **Tablet**: hybrid layout, sidebar navigation with collapsible menu, two-column dashboard, richer data tables with horizontal scroll if needed. **Desktop**: multi-column dashboard (3-column: available places, active requests, recent activity), left sidebar navigation always visible, data tables with sorting/filtering, hover states for additional context, inline PDF preview option.

interaction_patterns.name: Inline validation
interaction_patterns.description: Real-time form validation on blur. Error messages appear below field with red icon and descriptive text. Required fields marked with asterisk. Submit button disabled until all required fields valid. Success state: green checkmark icon. WCAG: error messages announced to screen readers, focus moves to first error field on submit attempt.

interaction_patterns.name: Progressive disclosure
interaction_patterns.description: Child dossier uses tabbed interface to manage complexity. Tabs: Info générale (always visible), Documents (expandable by category), Historique des placements (timeline), Rapports (filterable), Suivi scolaire/professionnel (accordion), Messagerie (threaded). External professionals see only tabs within their permission scope. Default: Info générale tab open. Unsaved changes prompt before tab switch.

interaction_patterns.name: Multi-target selection
interaction_patterns.description: Placement request form allows selecting multiple establishments via checkboxes after filtering by criteria (type, age range, gender, location, special needs). Selected establishments shown in summary panel. One placement request entity created, linked to all selected establishments. Each establishment sees request independently; social worker sees aggregated status view.

interaction_patterns.name: Persistent toast notifications
interaction_patterns.description: Toast notifications for real-time events (placement request received, report filed, message received). Appear top-right (desktop) or top-center (mobile), persist until user dismisses (no auto-dismiss for WCAG). Notification center icon in header shows unread count badge. Clicking opens dropdown with last 20 notifications, grouped by type, linked to relevant entity. Mark as read on click.

interaction_patterns.name: Drag-and-drop file upload
interaction_patterns.description: Drag-and-drop zone with browse fallback. Supported formats: PDF, DOCX, PNG, JPG (max 10MB per file). Upload progress indicator. Preview thumbnail for images, PDF icon for documents. Batch upload supported. Per-file status on failure (retry button). WCAG: keyboard upload via file input, upload status announced to screen readers.

interaction_patterns.name: Confirmation modals for destructive actions
interaction_patterns.description: Delete actions (document, user, establishment account suspension) require confirmation modal with explicit action description and Cancel/Confirm buttons. Soft delete with audit trail preservation. WCAG: focus trapped in modal, Escape key dismisses, confirmation announced to screen reader.

error_and_empty_states.context: Available places dashboard with no matching establishments
error_and_empty_states.description: Illustration (friendly, non-alarming), headline 'Aucune place disponible pour ces critères', body 'Essayez d'élargir vos filtres (âge, localisation, type d'établissement) ou contactez directement les établissements.', CTA 'Réinitialiser les filtres'. If zero places exist globally: headline 'Aucune place disponible actuellement', body 'Les établissements n'ont signalé aucune place libre. Vous pouvez créer une demande de placement et serez notifié(e) dès qu'une place se libère.'

error_and_empty_states.context: All establishments decline a placement request
error_and_empty_states.description: Status indicator 'Toutes les demandes refusées'. Notification sent to social worker. Detailed view shows decline reasons from each establishment (if provided). Actionable next steps: (1) 'Modifier le profil de l'enfant', (2) 'Relancer une nouvelle demande', (3) 'Contacter directement les établissements'. Empathetic tone acknowledging placement difficulty.

Inline error below upload area: 'Échec du téléversement' with specific reason (file too large >10MB, unsupported format, network error). Retry button. Batch upload: per-file status (success/failed), failed files remain in queue. WCAG: error announced, focus moves to error message.

error_and_empty_states.context: User attempts unauthorized action (e.g., external professional accessing restricted child dossier section)
error_and_empty_states.description: Modal or inline message: 'Accès refusé — Vous n'avez pas les permissions nécessaires pour cette action.' For external professionals: 'Cette section est réservée aux travailleurs sociaux du département. Contactez votre administrateur si vous avez besoin d'accéder à ces informations.' Admin contact link provided. WCAG: error announced, focus trapped in modal.

Top banner (persistent, dismissible): 'Connexion perdue — Certaines fonctionnalités peuvent être indisponibles. Vérifiez votre connexion Internet.' If user attempts action while offline: modal 'Action impossible hors ligne. Reconnectez-vous et réessayez.' No offline-first capability in V1. Auto-retry on reconnection.

error_and_empty_states.context: New or incomplete child dossier (empty Documents, Rapports, Messagerie tabs)
error_and_empty_states.description: Empty state in Documents: 'Aucun document téléversé', CTA 'Ajouter un document'. Rapports: 'Aucun rapport disponible — Les établissements n'ont pas encore déposé de compte-rendu.' Messagerie: 'Aucun message — Commencez une discussion avec les professionnels en charge de cet enfant', CTA 'Nouveau message'. Guidance-oriented, low-anxiety tone.

risks.description: External professionals (magistrates, doctors, teachers) require granular, role-based access within child dossiers. GDPR Art. 5 (data minimization) mandates limiting access to strictly necessary data. Over-permissive access violates GDPR; under-permissive access blocks legitimate professional needs. Permission matrix UI is a complex design problem (role × data section × action).
risks.mitigation: Explicit permission matrix UI for administrators with clear role-to-data mapping. Full audit trail on all external professional access (view, download, edit). Periodic access review prompts for administrators. Collaborate with data architect and legal to define exact data perimeter per role type.

risks.description: Placement matching relies on child profile vs establishment criteria (age, gender, needs confirmed; disability, behavioral profile, judicial constraints, geographic proximity mentioned but not exhaustive). Incomplete filter/matching UX may fail to surface relevant establishments, defeating core value proposition (faster placement search from 1-3 days to same day).
risks.mitigation: Iterative refinement with pilot department (Ain) to surface missing criteria. Placeholder filters in V1 for known criteria with extensible filter architecture. User feedback loop after pilot to add missing filters in V1.1.

risks.area: Adoption risk from varying digital literacy
risks.description: Establishments (especially familles d'accueil) and external professionals (family assistants, some educators) may have limited digital literacy. Current tool is Excel — platform is step-change in complexity. Poor onboarding or complex UX could lead to low adoption, jeopardizing pilot success with Ain department. Client has no change management or training plan.
risks.mitigation: Simple, guided onboarding flow with in-app tooltips and contextual help. Training materials (video tutorials, PDF guides) in French. Dedicated support channel (email/phone). Recommend client invest in change management plan and on-site training for pilot department. UX simplicity is critical — prioritize clarity over feature richness in V1.

risks.description: When multiple establishments accept a single placement request, the workflow for social worker to choose final placement is unclear. First-come-first-served, manual selection, or other logic? Ambiguity could lead to confusion or establishments feeling misled.
risks.mitigation: Design explicit workflow: social worker receives notifications of all acceptances, reviews each, manually selects one establishment, system auto-declines others with polite message ('Place was offered to another establishment'). Requires UX flow design and backend state machine.

blockers.decision: Exact data perimeter per external professional role type (magistrate, doctor, teacher, psychologist)
blockers.blocked_by: pm_users_roles_external has medium-severity doubt: 'Exact data access perimeter per external role type not yet defined — magistrates may need read-only judicial docs, doctors only health data, teachers only schooling data.' Cannot finalize child dossier tab visibility logic or permission matrix UI without this.

blockers.decision: Permission matrix UI design for administrator assigning granular access
blockers.blocked_by: ux_risk_external_professional_data_access flags that permission matrix is a complex UX problem (role × data section × action) not yet designed. Depends on finalized role-to-data mapping from PM and data architect.

blockers.decision: Multi-target placement request acceptance workflow when multiple establishments accept
blockers.blocked_by: ux_interaction_pattern_placement_request_multi_target has medium-severity doubt: 'Multi-target request acceptance workflow unclear — if multiple establishments accept, how does social worker choose? Manual selection, first-come-first-served, or other logic?' Requires PM decision on business rule.

audit.verdict_rationale: The pipeline contains several blocking inconsistencies, particularly around the undefined RBAC permission matrix and incomplete placement matching criteria, which affect multiple specialist designs and prevent reliable client presentation. While the overall scope is coherent, these gaps create fundamental doubts that invalidate the Field as a solid basis for proceeding without additional clarification.

inconsistencies.description: PM defines closed-by-default RBAC but does not specify exact permissions per external role, causing partial and speculative designs in backend, UX, and frontend implementations.
Placement matching criteria are listed as non-exhaustive by PM, blocking finalization of matching logic, UI patterns, and database indexing across specialists.
UX architect has not produced key journey tensions, making frontend designs speculative and dependent on unvalidated assumptions about user workflows.

scope_reality_check.assessment: Budget is unknown, but the scope involves complex multi-tenant architecture, GDPR audit trails, and 16+ component designs across four specialist areas, suggesting potentially high cost for a 16-month timeline.

discovery_questions.question: What is the total budget allocated for developing this child protection platform, including all phases up to launch in September 2026?
Can you provide the exact data access rules for each external professional role, such as what parts of a child's dossier a magistrate, doctor, teacher, or family assistant can view or edit?
What are all the specific criteria used to match children to placement establishments, beyond age, gender, and special needs, including any judicial or geographic constraints?
Is multi-factor authentication required for all users accessing the platform, especially those handling child health data?
Under French child protection law, what is the exact retention period for child records, and does it vary based on the type of case?
Can you confirm if the technology stack must include MongoDB with Prisma ORM, or if PostgreSQL is an acceptable alternative for better GDPR compliance?
What training and onboarding plan is in place for department workers, establishment staff, and external professionals to ensure adoption, especially for users with limited digital skills?
Can you provide or confirm the design for key user journeys, such as how social workers create placement requests or how establishments respond to them?

qa.reasoning_raw:
## Preparing for field analysis
