tensions.wants: Understand the sector and organizational context
tensions.value: Aide sociale à l'enfance (ASE) — departmental child protection service in France. Pilot: Département de l'Ain. Targets 1–10 departments at scale. Covers Métropole + DOM-TOM.

tensions.wants: Know which placement structures are in scope
Know current tooling for child records and placement tracking
tensions.value: Excel only — no dedicated software. No existing SSO or Active Directory. Each tool has its own credentials.

tensions.wants: Estimate data volume per department
tensions.value: 500 to 1500 children followed per department. <20 new placement requests per month.

tensions.wants: Know which external professionals need access

doubts.about: Magistrats and healthcare professionals have strict data access rules under French law — their role permissions will require careful legal scoping

tensions.wants: Understand the core pain point
tensions.value: Placement search takes 1–3 days per child. Workers must call all potential facilities manually. Establishments rarely proactively report available spots. Not GDPR compliant today.

tensions.wants: Understand GDPR non-compliance scope
tensions.value: Current Excel-based system is not GDPR compliant. Child records contain medical/health data (treatments, disability, psychological follow-up) — classified as special category data under GDPR Art. 9.

doubts.about: Health data requires explicit legal basis (Art. 9 GDPR), likely covered by child protection mission légale, but DPO involvement and DPIA will be mandatory

tensions.wants: Define GDPR compliance architecture for sensitive child data
tensions.value: Health and medical data present — GDPR Art. 9 special category. Requires: DPIA (Data Protection Impact Assessment), full audit trail with anonymization capability, role-based access control (closed by default), data retention policies per French child protection law (conservation until child's 25th birthday minimum).

doubts.about: Exact legal retention duration under French ASE law (CASF) needs validation — likely tied to child's majority + several years

tensions.wants: Define anonymizable audit trail requirements
tensions.value: Full audit log on all collections: create, update, delete, archive, document upload/download. Audit entries must be anonymizable (GDPR right to erasure) but structurally preserved for traceability. Implies soft-delete pattern + separate audit collection in MongoDB.

tensions.wants: Assess geographic compliance complexity
tensions.value: Platform covers DOM-TOM — French GDPR applies uniformly (RGPD + Loi Informatique et Libertés). No additional foreign data transfer complexity. Timezone handling required for DOM-TOM (UTC-4 to UTC+11).

doubts.about: Some DOM-TOM have specific child protection legislation adaptations — needs legal verification

tensions.wants: Define measurable success criteria
tensions.value: KPIs: time saved per placement search (currently 1–3 days → target same day), number of calls avoided, reduction in placement decision time. GDPR compliance achieved. Workers have more time for children and oversight of establishments.

tensions.wants: Define department-side user roles
tensions.value: Travailleurs sociaux du département: manage placement offers, track requests, manage child dossier, upload documents. Administrator: manage user accounts and access rights per department.

tensions.wants: Define establishment-side user roles
tensions.value: Établissements d'accueil: receive placement requests, complete child dossier with activity reports, schooling/professional tracking, signal available/unavailable places.

tensions.wants: Define external professional roles and access scope
tensions.value: External professionals (psychologists, doctors, teachers, magistrates, family assistants) need scoped read/write access. Closed by default, opened granularly via role/group management.

doubts.about: Exact data access perimeter per external role type not yet defined — magistrates may need read-only judicial docs, doctors only health data, teachers only schooling data

tensions.wants: Understand placement matching logic
tensions.value: Available place matching based on child profile vs establishment characteristics: age, gender, specific needs. Example: 12-year-old boy cannot be placed in a girls-only establishment. Multi-establishment simultaneous requests supported.

doubts.about: Full list of matching criteria (disability, behavioral profile, geographic proximity, judicial order constraints) not yet exhaustive

tensions.wants: Understand report submission and notification workflow
tensions.value: Establishments write reports/activity accounts. When submitted by establishment, the assigned caseworker (conseiller de suivi) receives an automatic notification. No formal validation/signature workflow — reports are informational entries.

tensions.wants: Define inter-professional communication features
value: Private internal messaging between professionals around a child
Automatic notifications and alerts (place freed, report filed, deadline approaching)
Discussion thread attached to child dossier

doubts.about: Messaging must be GDPR-compliant — messages mentioning child data are sensitive, storage and access rules apply

tensions.wants: Define document management scope
tensions.value: All child-related documents: judicial, medical, administrative (ID documents, etc.). Upload, download, generation from platform. Full GDPR traceability: who accessed, downloaded, uploaded, when.

doubts.about: Document generation templates (which documents, which format — PDF likely) not yet specified

tensions.wants: Confirm access scope boundaries
tensions.value: Platform is professionals-only. Children and families have no access. GDPR right of access requests handled offline.

tensions.wants: Define V1 must-have features
value: Report and activity entry by establishments
Messaging and discussion thread on child dossier

tensions.wants: Flag AI as future scope
tensions.value: AI features (automatic placement suggestions, dossier analysis, predictive alerts) explicitly deferred to a future version. No AI in V1.

tensions.wants: Know hard deadline
tensions.value: Target launch: September 2026.

tensions.wants: Know budget envelope
tensions.value: Not yet defined.

tensions.wants: Assess adoption risk
tensions.value: Main adoption risk: departments themselves (internal resistance to change). Secondary: establishments and family assistants (digital literacy concerns). No onboarding/training plan yet defined.

doubts.about: No support or change management plan — could jeopardize pilot success with Ain department

tensions.wants: Know integration requirements
tensions.value: No external system integrations required for V1. Standalone platform.

tensions.wants: Understand data migration scope
tensions.value: Partial Excel import: list of establishments and capacities, historical placements, list of social workers/users. Migration tooling required.

doubts.about: Data quality of Excel files unknown — likely inconsistent, will need cleaning and mapping logic

tensions.wants: Define security and access control architecture
tensions.value: Closed-by-default RBAC (Role-Based Access Control) with group management. All permissions denied by default, explicitly opened per role/group. Managed by department administrator. Implies permission matrix across roles × data collections × actions (read/write/delete/export).

tensions.wants: Infer authentication architecture
tensions.value: No Active Directory / SSO available. Email/password authentication via NextAuth.js. Invite-based user creation by department admin. MFA strongly recommended given sensitivity of child health data.

doubts.about: MFA requirement not confirmed by client — given GDPR Art. 9 health data, should be mandatory

tensions.wants: Infer core data model entities
value: Child (Enfant) — core entity with medical, judicial, schooling, professional data
Placement (Placement) — links child to establishment, with status and timeline
Establishment (Établissement) — with capacity, criteria, available places
PlacementRequest (DemandePlacement) — multi-target request with matching logic
Report (Rapport/CompteRendu) — filed by establishment or worker
Document — with metadata, access log, GDPR traceability

tensions.wants: Infer document storage architecture
tensions.value: Document upload/download requires cloud file storage — likely Vercel Blob or AWS S3. MongoDB stores metadata + access log only. Files must be access-controlled (no public URLs). GDPR deletion requires physical file removal capability.

doubts.about: File storage provider not yet decided — S3 or equivalent needed, not native to Vercel stack for large medical documents

tensions.wants: Assess real-time feature requirements
tensions.value: Real-time dashboard for available places and notifications requires either polling or WebSocket/SSE. Given Vercel serverless constraints, SSE or polling with short intervals is the pragmatic approach.

doubts.about: True real-time (WebSocket) on Vercel requires edge functions or external service — architect needs to evaluate

tensions.wants: Infer document generation requirements
tensions.value: PDF generation from platform (judicial, medical, administrative documents). Requires server-side PDF rendering — likely Puppeteer or PDFKit in Next.js API route. Templates not yet defined.

doubts.about: List of document templates and their data sources not yet specified — needs design phase

tensions.wants: Assess multi-tenant architecture needs
tensions.value: Platform designed for multiple departments — requires multi-tenant data isolation. Each department's data must be strictly separated. MongoDB tenant scoping via departmentId field or separate collections. Critical for GDPR compliance (data cannot leak across departments).

doubts.about: Multi-tenant architecture strategy (shared DB with tenant ID vs separate DB per tenant) needs data architect decision

tensions.wants: Quantify impact of placement search delay
tensions.value: Current placement search: 1 to 3 days per child. Less than 20 new requests per month per department. Cost: social worker time monopolized by phone calls instead of direct child support.

tensions.wants: Quantify legal and financial risk from GDPR non-compliance
tensions.value: Excel-based management of child health data represents significant GDPR exposure. Fines up to 4% of turnover or €20M under GDPR Art. 83. Reputational and political risk for the department given sensitivity of child protection data.

doubts.about: Client has not quantified the GDPR risk in financial terms — assumed high given health data + public institution

tensions.wants: Assess change management impact on project success
tensions.value: Primary adoption risk is the departments themselves. No training or change management plan exists. Pilot with Ain department — success is critical for multi-department rollout. Onboarding simplicity and UX quality are decisive factors.

tensions.wants: Assess scalability from pilot to multi-department
tensions.value: Starting with 1 department (Ain), designed to scale to multiple departments. Multi-tenant architecture mandatory from day one. Each department is a fully isolated tenant with its own users, establishments, children, and data.

tensions.wants: Document recruitment decisions
tensions.value: Recruiting lead_front (complex multi-role UI, dashboard, document viewer, messaging), lead_back (RBAC engine, placement matching, notifications, audit trail, file management), data_architect (multi-tenant MongoDB schema, GDPR audit log, anonymization, retention policies), ux_architect (multiple user profiles with varying digital literacy, critical child protection workflows). No ai_architect needed — AI explicitly deferred to V2.

tensions.wants: Define API architecture and conventions for the child protection platform
tensions.value: REST API via Next.js API Routes. Versioning: /api/v1/* prefix. Pagination: cursor-based for all list endpoints (limit + cursor params). Response envelope: { success: boolean, data: T | null, error?: { code, message }, meta?: { cursor, hasMore } }. Timestamp format: ISO 8601. All write operations return full updated entity. Multi-tenant: every API route enforces departmentId scoping via middleware.

doubts.about: Multi-tenant scoping enforcement pattern (middleware vs per-route) needs validation with data architect — must be fail-safe to prevent cross-department data leaks

tensions.wants: Define authentication and authorization architecture
tensions.value: NextAuth.js with Credentials provider (email/password). JWT session strategy (edge-compatible). MFA via TOTP (mandatory for users with health data access). Password policy: min 12 chars, uppercase, lowercase, number, symbol. Invite-based registration only (department admin creates user, generates invite link with 48h expiry). Session expiry: 8 hours. Refresh token rotation implemented. RBAC enforced at API layer via permission middleware checking user.role + user.groups against route + action + resource type.

doubts.about: MFA requirement assumed mandatory given GDPR Art. 9 health data — not explicitly confirmed by client (pm_security_auth marked as 'strongly recommended'). Needs confirmation before implementation.
Permission matrix (roles × resources × actions) not yet defined — requires collaboration with UX Architect and Data Architect to map all scoped access patterns per external professional type

tensions.wants: Define granular RBAC permission matrix across all roles and resources
tensions.value: Permission dimensions: role (department_admin, social_worker, establishment_user, psychologist, doctor, teacher, magistrate, family_assistant) × resource (child, placement, document, report, message, establishment, user) × action (read, create, update, delete, export, download). Default: all deny. Scoping rules: doctors access only health documents, magistrates only judicial documents, teachers only schooling data. Permissions stored in MongoDB Permission collection, evaluated per request via middleware.

doubts.about: Exact access perimeter per external role type not yet defined (pm_users_roles_external marked as medium doubt). Cannot finalize permission matrix without UX flow decisions and legal validation.
Group-based permissions (pm_security_rbac mentions 'group management') — unclear if groups override roles or compose with them. Needs clarification.

tensions.wants: Define document storage integration for child medical/judicial documents
tensions.value: AWS S3 (or S3-compatible) via @aws-sdk/client-s3. Bucket structure: {departmentId}/{childId}/{documentId}.{ext}. Pre-signed URLs for upload (15min expiry) and download (5min expiry, single-use). No public access. Metadata stored in MongoDB Document collection. GDPR deletion: physical S3 object delete + MongoDB soft-delete. File size limit: 50MB per document. Supported formats: PDF, JPG, PNG, DOCX.

doubts.about: Stack specifies MongoDB but pm_technical_file_storage suggests cloud file storage needed. S3 is not part of the fixed Next.js/Vercel stack — need confirmation that S3 integration is acceptable or if Vercel Blob is mandated.
Pre-signed URL strategy requires S3 credentials in Vercel environment variables — rotation and security posture not yet defined.

tensions.wants: Define email notification integration for placement requests, reports, and alerts
tensions.value: SendGrid for transactional email. Templates: placement_request_received, report_submitted, deadline_approaching, user_invite, password_reset. All emails link back to platform (no sensitive child data in email body per GDPR). Sent via Next.js API route /api/internal/send-email. Retry logic: 3 attempts with exponential backoff. Bounce/complaint webhooks stored in MongoDB EmailLog collection.

doubts.about: Email provider not specified in PM tensions. Assumed SendGrid as standard Vercel-compatible service. Needs confirmation or could switch to Resend, AWS SES, or other.

tensions.wants: Define server-side PDF generation for document export
tensions.value: @react-pdf/renderer for React-based PDF templates. Generation triggered via POST /api/v1/documents/generate. Template types: judicial_report, medical_summary, placement_history, activity_report. Rendered server-side in Next.js API route, uploaded to S3, returned as download URL. Templates defined as React components with typed data props.

doubts.about: pm_technical_document_generation mentions Puppeteer or PDFKit — @react-pdf/renderer chosen for better React/Next.js integration and type safety. Puppeteer requires headless Chrome, heavyweight for Vercel serverless. Needs architect approval.
Document templates not yet defined (pm_technical_document_generation medium doubt). Cannot implement generation endpoints until template data schemas are finalized.

tensions.wants: Define API-level security controls for GDPR-compliant child protection data
tensions.value: Rate limiting: 100 req/min per user (via upstash-ratelimit + Redis). Input validation: Zod schemas on all POST/PUT/PATCH bodies. CORS: restrictive, only frontend domain. Secrets: Vercel environment variables, rotated quarterly. HTTPS only. Helmet.js for security headers. CSRF protection via NextAuth CSRF token. All MongoDB queries use parameterized inputs (Prisma ORM prevents injection). Audit log middleware on every write operation. IP logging for all document downloads.

doubts.about: Stack specifies Prisma ORM but also MongoDB — Prisma's MongoDB support is production-ready but less mature than PostgreSQL. Need confirmation this is acceptable.
Rate limiting backend (Redis via Upstash) not part of fixed stack — need confirmation or alternative approach via Vercel Edge Config or in-memory store.

tensions.wants: Define error response conventions and logging strategy
tensions.value: Error response shape: { success: false, error: { code: string, message: string, details?: object } }. HTTP status codes: 400 (validation), 401 (auth), 403 (forbidden), 404 (not found), 409 (conflict), 500 (server error). Client-safe messages (no stack traces in production). Logging: structured JSON via Vercel logs, include userId, departmentId, route, timestamp, error type. Critical errors (GDPR violations, auth bypass attempts) trigger immediate alert via webhook to monitoring service.

tensions.wants: Implement anonymizable audit trail per GDPR requirement
tensions.value: AuditLog collection in MongoDB with fields: { id, timestamp, userId (nullable post-anonymization), departmentId, action (create/update/delete/read/export), resourceType, resourceId, changes (JSON diff for updates), ipAddress (nullable post-anonymization), userAgent }. Middleware intercepts all write operations and creates audit entry. Anonymization: on child GDPR erasure request, set userId and ipAddress to null but preserve action/timestamp for legal traceability. Soft-delete pattern: deleted entities marked with deletedAt, not physically removed, to preserve audit integrity.

tensions.wants: Implement real-time available places dashboard
tensions.value: Polling approach: frontend polls GET /api/v1/establishments/availability every 30 seconds. Vercel serverless constraints make WebSocket impractical. Endpoint returns cached availability data (updated on placement create/update/delete via event handler). Cache invalidation per departmentId. Alternative: Server-Sent Events (SSE) via Next.js API route with long-lived connection, but not recommended given Vercel execution time limits (10s hobby, 300s pro).

doubts.about: pm_technical_realtime flags SSE or WebSocket as needed but notes Vercel serverless constraints. Polling chosen as pragmatic default but may not meet 'real-time' user expectation. UX Architect should validate 30s refresh is acceptable.
If true real-time (<5s latency) is required, would need external service (Pusher, Ably) or Vercel Edge Functions with SSE — both outside fixed stack. Would be a blocker.

tensions.wants: Implement placement request matching logic
tensions.value: Matching algorithm in /api/v1/placements/match endpoint. Inputs: childProfile (age, gender, needs array), establishmentCriteria filters. Logic: filter establishments by capacity > 0, age range compatibility, gender restrictions, special needs support flags. Return ranked list of matching establishments. Multi-establishment requests: create multiple PlacementRequest records with same requestId. Notifications sent to all targeted establishments. Business rules encoded in MatchingService class, unit-tested.

doubts.about: pm_business_rules_placement_matching notes full matching criteria not yet exhaustive (disability, behavioral profile, geographic proximity, judicial order constraints). Cannot finalize matching logic until complete criteria list is defined.

tensions.wants: Ensure inter-professional messaging is GDPR-compliant
tensions.value: Message collection stores all professional communications. Fields: { id, threadId, childId, authorId, recipientIds, content (encrypted at rest), timestamp, readBy, attachments (file references) }. Encryption: AES-256 via libsodium, keys stored in Vercel secrets. Access control: only users with read permission on childId can access thread. Audit trail: all message reads/writes logged. GDPR erasure: messages related to erased child are anonymized (content replaced with '[ANONYMIZED]', authorId nulled).

doubts.about: pm_business_rules_communication flags messaging must be GDPR-compliant but encryption-at-rest not explicitly required. Assumed necessary given health data context. Needs confirmation — encryption adds complexity.

tensions.wants: Implement Excel import tooling for migration
tensions.value: Admin-only endpoint POST /api/v1/admin/import with file upload. Parses Excel via xlsx library. Mapping logic: establishments sheet → Establishment collection, users sheet → User collection (with invite emails sent), placements sheet → historical Placement records. Validation: schema validation per row, error report generated. Dry-run mode supported. Import job runs asynchronously, status tracked in ImportJob collection.

doubts.about: pm_data_import notes data quality likely inconsistent, will need cleaning and mapping logic. Cannot finalize import schema until sample Excel files reviewed. Should be handled in data architecture phase.

tensions.wants: Implement event-driven notification system
tensions.value: Notification collection stores in-app notifications. Event handlers trigger on: placement request received, report submitted, deadline approaching, message received, available place freed. Notifications created via NotificationService, linked to user + child. API endpoints: GET /api/v1/notifications (paginated, unread count), PATCH /api/v1/notifications/:id/read. Email notifications triggered in parallel via back_integration_email_notifications.

tensions.wants: Flag missing data schema definitions blocking API implementation
tensions.value: Cannot finalize API request/response shapes without complete MongoDB schema definitions for: Child (medical/judicial/schooling fields), Placement (status enum, timeline structure), Establishment (criteria fields for matching), Document (metadata fields), Report (structure and validation). Data Architect must define schemas before API contracts are final.

tensions.wants: Flag missing UX flow definitions affecting API design
tensions.value: Several API flows cannot be finalized without UX decisions: placement request multi-step wizard (affects POST /api/v1/placements shape), document viewer access control UX (affects download URL generation strategy), messaging thread UI (affects polling vs push notification approach). UX Architect must define these flows.

tensions.wants: Challenge stack compatibility: Prisma ORM with MongoDB
tensions.value: Fixed stack specifies both 'MongoDB' and 'Prisma ORM'. Prisma's MongoDB connector is production-ready but has limitations vs PostgreSQL: no transactions across collections, no full-text search, limited aggregation pipeline support. Given GDPR audit requirements and multi-tenant scoping complexity, PostgreSQL would be safer. Recommend clarifying if MongoDB is mandatory or if PostgreSQL substitution is acceptable.

tensions.wants: Define user journey for department social workers managing placement requests
tensions.value: Department social worker (travailleur social) managing a child dossier and placement search. Key steps: (1) Create/access child dossier, (2) View available places dashboard with filters (age, gender, special needs, location), (3) Create placement request for one or multiple establishments, (4) Track request status, (5) Receive notifications when establishment responds, (6) Complete child dossier with documents and notes, (7) Review reports filed by establishments, (8) Communicate with professionals via messaging thread.

doubts.about: Full filter/search criteria for placement matching not exhaustively defined — age, gender, disability confirmed; behavioral profile, judicial constraints, geographic proximity mentioned but not detailed

tensions.wants: Define user journey for establishment workers receiving and responding to placement requests
tensions.value: Establishment user (MECS, foyer, famille d'accueil coordinator) receives placement request notification. Key steps: (1) View placement request with child profile summary, (2) Review child dossier (scoped to relevant fields — age, gender, needs; medical data may be restricted), (3) Accept or decline request with optional note, (4) Update available/unavailable places in establishment profile, (5) File activity reports and compte-rendus for placed children, (6) Upload documents related to child follow-up (schooling, therapy, etc.), (7) Receive notifications from caseworker or other professionals.

doubts.about: Establishment user access to child medical/health data is unclear — some data (disability, treatments) may be needed for placement decision, but access must be GDPR-minimized
Workflow for place availability update (manual signal vs calendar-based) not specified

tensions.wants: Define user journey for department administrator managing users and access rights
tensions.value: Department administrator manages user accounts, roles, and access rights. Key steps: (1) Invite new users (social workers, external professionals) via email, (2) Assign roles and groups (closed-by-default RBAC), (3) Grant/revoke granular permissions for external professionals (read-only access to specific child dossier sections), (4) View audit log for compliance review, (5) Manage establishment accounts (create, suspend), (6) Configure department-level settings (notification preferences, document templates).

doubts.about: Permission matrix UI (which roles can access which data collections and actions) is complex — design pattern for granular permission assignment not yet chosen

tensions.wants: Define user journey for external professionals with scoped read/write access
tensions.value: External professional (psychologist, teacher, doctor, magistrate, family assistant) invited to access specific child dossier or section. Key steps: (1) Receive invitation email with scoped access link, (2) Authenticate (MFA required), (3) View assigned child dossier sections (scoped by role — magistrate sees judicial docs, doctor sees health data, teacher sees schooling), (4) Upload documents or reports within permitted scope, (5) Read/write in messaging thread if granted, (6) No access to placement request workflow or available places dashboard.

doubts.about: Exact data perimeter per external role type not yet defined — magistrates may need judicial docs only, doctors health data only, teachers schooling only
Invitation workflow UX (single-use link vs permanent account, token expiry, re-invitation flow) not specified

tensions.wants: Define navigation structure and page hierarchy
tensions.value: Top-level navigation: (1) Tableau de bord (Dashboard) — available places, active placement requests, recent notifications; (2) Dossiers enfants (Child Dossiers) — searchable list, detail view with tabs (Info générale, Documents, Historique des placements, Rapports, Suivi scolaire/professionnel, Messagerie); (3) Demandes de placement (Placement Requests) — create, track, manage; (4) Établissements (Establishments) — directory, profiles, capacity overview; (5) Messagerie (Messaging) — child-centered threads; (6) Administration (Admin-only) — user management, roles, audit log, settings. Mobile: bottom tab navigation. Desktop: left sidebar navigation with collapsible sections.

doubts.about: Child dossier tab organization may need further refinement based on external professional access patterns — some tabs may be conditionally visible

tensions.wants: Define WCAG compliance level and specific accessibility constraints
tensions.value: Target: WCAG 2.1 Level AA. Requirements: (1) Keyboard navigation for all interactions (placement request form, document upload, filters), (2) Screen reader support — semantic HTML, ARIA labels for dynamic content (notifications, real-time dashboard updates), (3) Color contrast ratio ≥4.5:1 for text, ≥3:1 for UI components, (4) Focus indicators visible on all interactive elements, (5) Form validation messages announced to screen readers, (6) PDF documents must be accessible (tagged PDFs), (7) Time-based notifications must not auto-dismiss (users can dismiss manually), (8) Alternative text for all document icons and status indicators.

doubts.about: Client has not explicitly requested WCAG AA, but public sector platform in France typically requires it — assumption based on sector norms

tensions.wants: Define mobile vs desktop priorities and breakpoint rationale
tensions.value: Primary platform: Desktop (social workers spend most time at desk), but mobile is critical for establishments and external professionals (psychologists, family assistants on the move). Responsive strategy: desktop-first design, mobile-optimized for key flows (view placement requests, file reports, upload documents, check notifications). Breakpoints: mobile (<768px), tablet (768–1024px), desktop (≥1024px). Mobile: simplified dashboard, single-column layout, bottom tab navigation, touch-optimized inputs (date pickers, file upload). Desktop: multi-column dashboard, sidebar navigation, richer data tables with sorting/filtering.

tensions.wants: Define form validation interaction pattern
tensions.value: Inline validation with real-time feedback. Child dossier and placement request forms validate fields on blur. Error messages appear below field with icon. Required fields marked with asterisk. Submit button disabled until all required fields valid. Success state: green checkmark icon. WCAG: error messages announced to screen readers, focus moves to first error field on submit attempt.

tensions.wants: Define progressive disclosure pattern for complex child dossier
tensions.value: Child dossier uses tabbed interface with progressive disclosure. Tabs: Info générale (always visible), Documents (expandable by category: judicial, medical, administrative), Historique des placements (timeline view), Rapports (filterable by date/author), Suivi scolaire/professionnel (accordion sections), Messagerie (threaded view). External professionals see only tabs within their permission scope. Default view: Info générale tab open. Unsaved changes prompt before tab switch.

doubts.about: Tab visibility logic for external professionals depends on permission matrix not yet fully defined

tensions.wants: Define notification interaction pattern
tensions.value: Toast notifications for real-time events (placement request received, report filed, message received). Appear top-right (desktop) or top-center (mobile), persist until dismissed (no auto-dismiss for WCAG). Notification center icon in header shows unread count (badge). Clicking opens dropdown with recent notifications (last 20), grouped by type. Link to relevant entity (child dossier, placement request, message thread). Mark as read on click. Persistent notifications stored in database, queryable by user.

tensions.wants: Define document upload and management interaction pattern
tensions.value: Drag-and-drop file upload with browse fallback. Supported formats: PDF, DOCX, PNG, JPG (max 10MB per file). Upload progress indicator. Preview thumbnail for images, PDF icon for documents. Document list with columns: name, type (judicial/medical/administrative), uploaded by, date, actions (view, download, delete). GDPR audit: who uploaded, who accessed (view/download), when. Delete requires confirmation modal (soft delete, audit trail preserved). WCAG: keyboard upload via file input, upload status announced to screen readers.

doubts.about: Document preview (inline PDF viewer vs external download) not yet decided — inline viewer improves UX but adds complexity

tensions.wants: Define interaction pattern for multi-establishment placement requests
tensions.value: Placement request form: (1) Select child from dropdown (or create new), (2) Enter placement urgency (routine/urgent), (3) Filter establishments by criteria (type, age range, gender, location, special needs capacity), (4) Select one or multiple establishments from filtered list (checkbox selection), (5) Add optional note/context, (6) Submit. Result: one placement request entity linked to multiple establishments. Each establishment sees request independently. Status tracked per establishment (pending/accepted/declined). Social worker sees aggregated status view.

doubts.about: Multi-target request acceptance workflow unclear — if multiple establishments accept, how does social worker choose? Manual selection, first-come-first-served, or other logic?

tensions.wants: Define empty state for available places dashboard when no places match criteria
tensions.value: Empty state: illustration (friendly, non-alarming), headline 'Aucune place disponible pour ces critères', body text 'Essayez d'élargir vos filtres (âge, localisation, type d'établissement) ou contactez directement les établissements.', CTA button 'Réinitialiser les filtres'. If no places exist at all (not just filtered out): headline 'Aucune place disponible actuellement', body 'Les établissements n'ont signalé aucune place libre. Vous pouvez créer une demande de placement et serez notifié(e) dès qu'une place se libère.'

tensions.wants: Define error state when all establishments decline a placement request
tensions.value: Status indicator: 'Toutes les demandes refusées'. Notification sent to social worker. Detailed view shows decline reasons from each establishment (if provided). Actionable next steps: (1) 'Modifier le profil de l'enfant' (edit child profile to adjust needs/criteria), (2) 'Relancer une nouvelle demande' (create new request to different establishments), (3) 'Contacter directement les établissements' (escalate offline). Empathetic tone — acknowledges difficulty of placement search.

tensions.wants: Define error state for failed document upload
tensions.value: Inline error below upload area: 'Échec du téléversement' with specific reason (file too large >10MB, unsupported format, network error). Retry button. If upload partially succeeds in batch upload, show per-file status (success/failed). Failed files remain in upload queue for retry. WCAG: error announced to screen reader, focus moves to error message.

tensions.wants: Define error state when user attempts unauthorized action
tensions.value: Modal or inline message: 'Accès refusé — Vous n'avez pas les permissions nécessaires pour cette action.' For external professionals attempting to access restricted child dossier sections: 'Cette section est réservée aux travailleurs sociaux du département. Contactez votre administrateur si vous avez besoin d'accéder à ces informations.' No technical error codes shown to user. Admin contact link provided. WCAG: error announced, focus trapped in modal.

tensions.wants: Define error state for network failure or offline mode
tensions.value: Top banner (persistent, dismissible): 'Connexion perdue — Certaines fonctionnalités peuvent être indisponibles. Vérifiez votre connexion Internet.' If user attempts action while offline: modal 'Action impossible hors ligne. Reconnectez-vous et réessayez.' No offline-first capability in V1 (complex given GDPR audit requirements). Auto-retry on reconnection for pending actions.

tensions.wants: Define empty state for new or incomplete child dossier
tensions.value: Empty state in Documents tab: 'Aucun document téléversé', CTA 'Ajouter un document'. Empty state in Rapports tab: 'Aucun rapport disponible — Les établissements n'ont pas encore déposé de compte-rendu.' Empty state in Messagerie: 'Aucun message — Commencez une discussion avec les professionnels en charge de cet enfant', CTA 'Nouveau message'. Guidance-oriented, low-anxiety tone.

tensions.wants: Flag risk around scoped data access for external professionals
tensions.value: External professionals (magistrates, doctors, teachers) require granular, role-based data access within child dossiers. GDPR Art. 5 (data minimization) requires limiting access to strictly necessary data. Risk: over-permissive access could violate GDPR; under-permissive access could block legitimate professional needs. Mitigation: explicit permission matrix UI for admin, audit trail on all external professional access, periodic access review prompts.

doubts.about: Permission matrix not yet designed — complex UX problem (role × data section × action)

tensions.wants: Flag risk that placement matching filter criteria may be under-specified
tensions.value: Placement matching relies on child profile vs establishment criteria (age, gender, needs). Client mentioned additional criteria (disability, behavioral profile, judicial constraints, geographic proximity) but not exhaustively defined. Risk: incomplete filter/matching UX may fail to surface relevant establishments, defeating core value proposition (faster placement search). Mitigation: iterative refinement with pilot department (Ain), placeholder filters in V1 for known criteria, extensible filter architecture.

tensions.wants: Flag adoption risk from varying digital literacy across user groups
tensions.value: Establishments (especially familles d'accueil) and external professionals (family assistants, some educators) may have limited digital literacy. Current tool is Excel — platform represents step-change in complexity. Risk: poor onboarding or complex UX could lead to low adoption, jeopardizing pilot success with Ain. Mitigation: simple, guided onboarding flow, in-app tooltips and contextual help, training materials (videos, guides), support channel (email/phone). No change management plan yet defined by client — high-severity gap.

tensions.wants: Design normalized MongoDB schema for Child, Placement, PlacementRequest, Establishment, Report, Document, User, Message, AuditLog, Notification with multi-tenant isolation
tensions.value: Schema designed around 10 core collections: Child, Placement, PlacementRequest, Establishment, Report, Document, User, Message, AuditLog, Notification. All operational collections include departmentId for multi-tenant isolation. Child contains sensitive health data (treatments, disabilities, psychological follow-up) and must be treated as GDPR Art. 9 special category. Soft-delete pattern applied to Child, Placement, Document to preserve audit trail integrity.

doubts.about: Full field list for Child entity depends on exhaustive matching criteria (disability types, behavioral profile, judicial constraints) not yet documented
Message collection structure depends on whether messaging is chat-like or threaded discussion — UX decision pending

tensions.wants: Choose multi-tenant isolation strategy: shared collections with departmentId filter vs separate databases per department
tensions.value: Shared MongoDB database with departmentId field in every operational collection. Filter enforcement in middleware layer. Rationale: simplifies deployment (Vercel MongoDB Atlas single connection), cost-effective for 1–10 departments with modest data volumes (500–1500 children each). Indexes include departmentId as first compound key for query isolation and performance.

doubts.about: Shared database increases risk of accidental cross-tenant data leakage if filter logic is bypassed — requires strict backend middleware enforcement and automated tests

tensions.wants: Define compound indexes to enforce tenant isolation and query performance
tensions.value: All queries must filter by departmentId first. Compound indexes on (departmentId, <query_field>) for Child, Placement, PlacementRequest, Establishment, Report, Document, Message. Unique indexes include departmentId where logical uniqueness is tenant-scoped (e.g., User email unique within department only).

tensions.wants: Design indexes for placement matching queries (child profile vs establishment criteria)
tensions.value: Placement matching queries filter Establishment by availability, age range, gender acceptance, special needs accommodation. Requires compound index on Establishment: (departmentId, availableCapacity > 0, ageMin, ageMax, genderAccepted, specialNeeds). Query pattern is read-heavy, low write frequency (<20 new requests/month/dept).

doubts.about: Full matching criteria schema not yet exhaustive — disability types, behavioral profiles, geographic proximity, judicial constraints may require additional indexed fields

tensions.wants: Design anonymizable audit log structure that preserves traceability after GDPR erasure
tensions.value: Separate AuditLog collection with fields: id, timestamp, departmentId, collection, documentId, action (create/update/delete/read/download), userId (foreign key to User), userPseudonym (anonymizable hash), ip, changes (JSON diff). When child is anonymized via GDPR right to erasure: Child.personalData fields are redacted, but AuditLog entries remain with userPseudonym instead of userId. Traceability preserved, personal identity removed.

doubts.about: GDPR erasure scope unclear: does erasure apply to the child only, or also to professional users who accessed the child? Likely child only, but legal confirmation needed

tensions.wants: Define retention duration for Child records under French ASE law (CASF)
tensions.value: French child protection law (Code de l'Action Sociale et des Familles — CASF) requires retention of child records until the child reaches age 25 (Art. L221-1 and related regulations). Implemented as TTL: Child.retentionUntil = child.dateOfBirth + 25 years. MongoDB TTL index on Child.retentionUntil triggers automatic deletion. Related Placement, Report, Document records cascade-deleted or anonymized.

doubts.about: Exact retention duration under CASF not confirmed by client DPO — assumed 25 years (majority + several years), but could be longer for certain judicial cases
Cascade deletion vs anonymization strategy for related records (Placement, Report, Document) not yet defined — anonymization may be safer for audit purposes

tensions.wants: Define retention duration for AuditLog collection
tensions.value: AuditLog must be retained longer than operational data for legal traceability (public institution requirement). Proposed: 10 years retention for AuditLog (standard French public record retention). TTL index on AuditLog.timestamp. Anonymization applied after Child erasure, but log structure preserved.

doubts.about: 10-year retention for audit logs not confirmed by client legal team — may need alignment with French public archives regulation

tensions.wants: Define retention for uploaded documents (judicial, medical, administrative files)
tensions.value: Documents follow the same retention as Child records: retentionUntil = child.dateOfBirth + 25 years. Document metadata stored in MongoDB, physical files in cloud storage (S3 or Vercel Blob). GDPR deletion requires both metadata deletion and physical file removal from cloud storage. Lifecycle policy on cloud bucket must match MongoDB TTL.

doubts.about: Cloud storage provider not yet selected — S3 lifecycle policies supported, Vercel Blob retention automation unclear

tensions.wants: Define migration tooling for Excel import (establishments, historical placements, users)
tensions.value: Migration via Node.js scripts using xlsx library. Three import stages: (1) Establishment list with capacities, (2) User list (social workers), (3) Historical placements (optional, for reporting continuity). Data cleaning layer required (normalize names, validate emails, map inconsistent establishment IDs). Dry-run validation mode before production import. No automated sync — one-time migration only.

doubts.about: Excel file quality unknown — may contain duplicates, inconsistent establishment naming, missing fields. Cleaning logic will be iterative based on actual pilot data

tensions.wants: Define schema change management strategy for production post-launch
tensions.value: Schema changes managed via Prisma migrations (if using Prisma with MongoDB) or custom migration scripts stored in /migrations folder with timestamps. Migrations applied on deploy via CI/CD pre-deploy hook. MongoDB schema flexibility allows additive changes (new fields) without downtime. Breaking changes (field renames, deletions) require two-phase deploy: (1) add new field + backfill, (2) deprecate old field. Rollback plan: maintain last known-good schema snapshot.

doubts.about: ORM choice not yet decided by backend lead — Prisma vs Mongoose vs native MongoDB driver affects migration tooling

tensions.wants: Define query patterns for real-time available places dashboard
tensions.value: Dashboard aggregates available capacity per establishment, filtered by departmentId. Query: Establishment.find({ departmentId, availableCapacity > 0 }).sort(lastUpdated). Low frequency writes (establishments update capacity manually), high frequency reads (dashboard polling every 30–60s per user). Index on (departmentId, availableCapacity, lastUpdated). Caching layer optional but not critical given low data volume per tenant.

tensions.wants: Define query patterns for messaging/discussion threads attached to child dossier
tensions.value: Message collection schema: childId (foreign key), departmentId, authorId, timestamp, content, readBy[]. Query pattern: fetch all messages for a child dossier, ordered by timestamp. Compound index on (childId, timestamp). Read-heavy. Notification triggers when new message posted. No edit/delete functionality in V1 (audit compliance).

doubts.about: Message edit/delete policy unclear — GDPR right to erasure may require message anonymization, but audit trail must be preserved

tensions.wants: Flag risk of accidental cross-tenant data exposure
tensions.value: Shared database with departmentId filtering creates risk of data leakage if backend query logic omits departmentId filter. Mitigation: (1) middleware enforces departmentId injection on all queries, (2) automated integration tests verify tenant isolation, (3) MongoDB pre-query hooks (if using Mongoose) enforce departmentId presence. Severity: blocking for GDPR compliance.

tensions.wants: Flag risk of unauthorized document access via direct cloud storage URLs
tensions.value: Documents stored in S3/Vercel Blob must not be accessible via public URLs. All file access must route through backend API with RBAC check. Mitigation: (1) S3 bucket private with presigned URLs generated on-demand, (2) backend validates user permission before generating presigned URL, (3) AuditLog records all document downloads. Severity: blocking for GDPR compliance.

doubts.about: Vercel Blob access control model not yet validated — may not support presigned URLs, requiring proxy download through API route

tensions.wants: Flag risk of poor data quality in Excel migration files
tensions.value: Excel files likely contain duplicates, inconsistent naming, missing fields, outdated records. Poor import data quality will undermine pilot success. Mitigation: (1) dry-run validation with error reporting before production import, (2) manual data cleaning phase with client, (3) fallback: start with empty database and manual data entry if import fails. Severity: medium (affects pilot timeline but not architecture).

tensions.wants: Flag gap: detailed RBAC permission matrix per role not yet defined
tensions.value: PM identified closed-by-default RBAC with role/group management, but exact permissions per role (e.g., magistrate can read judicial docs only, doctor can read/write health data only, teacher can read schooling data only) are not documented. Backend lead will need this matrix to implement permission checks. Recommend: create permission matrix spreadsheet mapping roles × collections × actions (read/write/delete/export).

tensions.wants: Flag gap: document generation templates not yet specified
tensions.value: PM mentions PDF generation from platform (judicial, medical, administrative documents), but templates not yet designed. Backend lead will need template definitions (data sources, formatting, legal boilerplate). Recommend: UX/product design phase for document templates before backend implementation.

tensions.wants: Define frontend component structure and patterns
tensions.value: Feature-based component architecture: /app for Next.js App Router pages, /components split into /ui (shadcn/ui primitives + Tailwind-styled atoms), /features (domain-specific composite components like ChildDossier, PlacementRequestForm, AvailablePlacesDashboard), /layouts (RoleBasedLayout with RBAC-aware navigation). Shared state via React Context for auth session and user permissions. Server Components by default, Client Components ('use client') only when interactivity or hooks required (forms, modals, real-time polling).

doubts.about: UX architect has not yet produced ux_* tensions defining user journeys and screen flows — component granularity and page structure partially speculative
Real-time notification mechanism (polling vs SSE) not finalized by backend architect — affects NotificationBell and AvailablePlacesDashboard component design

tensions.wants: Define Next.js App Router structure and route protection
tensions.value: App Router with role-based route groups: /app/(auth) for login/invite flows, /app/(department) for social workers and admins, /app/(establishment) for establishment users, /app/(external) for external professionals. Middleware at /middleware.ts intercepts all routes, validates NextAuth session, checks user role, redirects to appropriate dashboard or /auth/login if unauthorized. Dynamic routes: /department/children/[childId], /department/placements/[requestId], /establishment/requests/[requestId]. Multi-tenant isolation enforced at middleware layer via session.user.departmentId.

doubts.about: External professional role access patterns not yet defined by PM or UX architect — route structure for /app/(external) is placeholder pending clarity on whether external users see filtered child lists or only specific assigned children
Admin panel scope unclear — does department admin manage users via dedicated /admin routes or inline within department dashboard? Affects route hierarchy.

tensions.wants: Define client-side state management strategy
tensions.value: Minimal client state via React Server Components by default. NextAuth session via useSession hook for auth state. TanStack Query (React Query) for server state caching and optimistic updates on mutations (child dossier edits, placement request submission, report filing). Zustand for ephemeral UI state only (modal open/close, sidebar expanded, filter state on dashboards). No global Redux — complexity not justified given server-heavy architecture. Forms managed via React Hook Form + Zod validation schemas shared with backend.

doubts.about: Real-time notification delivery mechanism unclear — if SSE or WebSocket adopted, will require additional state management layer (e.g., Zustand store for notification queue) beyond React Query
Messaging/discussion thread state management depends on backend real-time architecture — optimistic updates via React Query may be insufficient if true collaborative editing required

tensions.wants: Define NextAuth.js integration and session management
tensions.value: NextAuth.js v5 (Auth.js) with Credentials provider for email/password login. Session strategy: JWT stored in httpOnly cookie. Custom pages: /auth/login, /auth/invite (invite token validation + password setup). Middleware protects all routes except /auth/*. Session payload includes: userId, email, role (department_worker | department_admin | establishment_user | external_professional), departmentId (for multi-tenant isolation), permissions array (for RBAC checks). MFA via TOTP (e.g., speakeasy + qrcode) recommended but not yet confirmed as V1 requirement. Invite-based user creation: department admin generates invite link via API route, sends via email, user sets password on first login.

doubts.about: MFA requirement not confirmed — PM flagged it as recommended but client has not committed. If mandatory, affects /auth/mfa-setup and /auth/mfa-verify flows and session payload structure.
External professional authentication flow unclear — do external users receive invite links like internal users, or is there a separate registration flow? Affects /auth/invite logic.
Session refresh strategy on long-lived sessions (GDPR audit context requires tracking active sessions) — JWT expiration and refresh token rotation not yet designed.

tensions.wants: Define document upload, viewer, and GDPR audit integration
tensions.value: Document management via file upload component (react-dropzone) with client-side validation (file type, size limit). Upload triggers POST to /api/documents with multipart/form-data, backend returns signed URL for download. Document viewer: inline PDF viewer via react-pdf or browser native <embed>, image preview for scans, download button for all types. Every document action (upload, view, download) triggers audit log entry via backend API. GDPR compliance: document list shows access history (who viewed/downloaded, when) visible to department admin. Soft delete: documents marked deleted but not physically removed until GDPR retention period expires.

doubts.about: Document generation templates (PDF creation from platform) not yet defined by PM — unclear which documents are generated vs uploaded, affects UI for 'Generate Document' vs 'Upload Document' flows.
File storage backend (Vercel Blob vs S3) not finalized — affects URL signing strategy and download flow. If S3, presigned URLs; if Vercel Blob, different API.
Document viewer accessibility for low digital literacy users (establishment staff, family assistants) — may need simplified UI or guided upload flow, depends on UX research.

tensions.wants: Define how RBAC permissions are enforced in UI components
tensions.value: Permission checks via custom React hook usePermissions(resource, action) wrapping session permissions array. Component-level conditionals: {hasPermission('child', 'edit') && <EditButton />}. Route-level guards in middleware validate role + permissions before rendering page. Navigation menu dynamically filtered based on user role: department workers see Placements + Children, establishments see Requests + Reports, admins see Users + Settings. Optimistic UI: forms submit immediately, show loading state, revert on 403 Forbidden from backend. Error boundary catches unauthorized access attempts, shows 'Contact admin for access' message.

doubts.about: Granular permission matrix (which role can perform which action on which resource) not yet fully defined by backend architect or PM — usePermissions hook logic partially speculative.

External professional permissions highly variable by individual assignment (e.g., psychologist assigned to specific child) — may require dynamic per-user permission fetch rather than role-based lookup, affects usePermissions architecture.

tensions.wants: Define client-side notification delivery and display
tensions.value: Notification system: NotificationBell component in app header, polls /api/notifications every 30s (fallback if no SSE). Unread count badge. Dropdown shows last 10 notifications with timestamp, read/unread status, action link (e.g., 'View placement request'). Clicking notification marks as read, navigates to relevant page. Toast notifications (react-hot-toast) for real-time alerts when user is active (e.g., 'New placement request received'). Notification types: placement_request_received, report_submitted, deadline_approaching, place_freed. Backend delivers notification list via GET /api/notifications?unread=true, marks read via PATCH /api/notifications/:id.

doubts.about: Real-time delivery mechanism (polling vs SSE) not finalized — 30s polling interval is fallback assumption, may be too slow for urgent placement requests. If SSE adopted, NotificationBell needs EventSource integration.
Notification preferences (email vs in-app, notification type filters) not in V1 scope per PM, but users may expect basic on/off toggle — affects SettingsPage if added.

tensions.wants: Define UI for placement request creation and matching
tensions.value: Placement request flow: 3-step wizard (CreatePlacementRequestWizard). Step 1: Select child from dropdown (department workers only see children in their department via multi-tenant filter). Step 2: Define placement criteria (age range, gender, special needs checkboxes, urgency level). Step 3: View matched establishments list (MatchedEstablishmentsList component shows compatibility score if backend provides it, otherwise simple filter match), select one or multiple targets, submit request. Backend returns match results via POST /api/placements/match with establishment IDs. Submitted requests appear in /department/placements list with status badges (pending, accepted, rejected). Establishments see incoming requests at /establishment/requests, can accept/reject with optional message.

doubts.about: Placement matching algorithm (how compatibility score is calculated, which criteria are weighted) not yet defined by backend architect — MatchedEstablishmentsList may only show binary match/no-match, or may show ranked list. UI design depends on algorithm output.

Multi-establishment simultaneous request UX not yet designed — does user submit one request to multiple establishments at once, or create separate requests? Affects wizard Step 3 selection UI (checkboxes vs radio buttons).
Establishment acceptance workflow unclear — can establishment propose alternative placement dates, or is it accept/reject only? Affects request detail page interaction.

tensions.wants: Define messaging/discussion thread UI on child dossier
tensions.value: Messaging thread: MessageThread component embedded in child dossier page (/department/children/[childId]). Displays chronological list of messages with author name, role badge, timestamp. Composer at bottom (textarea + Send button). Messages stored per child, visible to all professionals with access to that child (RBAC filtered by backend). No real-time updates in V1 — user manually refreshes or page refetch on navigation. Optimistic update: new message appears immediately, queued for backend POST, shows error toast if send fails. Message edit/delete not in V1 scope (GDPR audit trail complexity).

doubts.about: Message access control for external professionals (e.g., should psychologist see all messages or only messages tagged to them?) not yet defined by PM — affects MessageThread query filter logic.
Message attachments (e.g., attach document to message) not mentioned in PM scope but users may expect it — affects Composer UI if added.
No real-time updates in V1 means users won't see new messages from other professionals without refresh — could cause coordination issues, may need to revisit if pilot feedback demands it.

tensions.wants: Define real-time dashboard for available placement spots
tensions.value: AvailablePlacesDashboard: table view of all establishments with current capacity (total spots, occupied, available). Columns: establishment name, type (MECS, Foyer, etc.), location, age range, gender, available spots count, last updated timestamp. Filters: establishment type, location, age range, gender. Sort by available spots (descending) or last updated. Establishment users update availability via modal (UpdateCapacityModal) on their dashboard, triggers immediate refresh on department worker dashboard via polling or SSE. Color coding: green if spots available, red if full, yellow if nearly full (threshold TBD). Refresh button for manual update.

doubts.about: Real-time update mechanism (polling vs SSE) not finalized — affects dashboard refresh strategy. 30s polling may be acceptable given <20 requests/month volume, but users expect 'real-time' per PM scope.

Capacity update workflow unclear — do establishments update total capacity (e.g., 10 spots) and system calculates available = total - occupied, or do they manually set available count? Affects UpdateCapacityModal form fields.
Nearly full threshold (yellow indicator) not defined — arbitrary UX decision, may need PM or UX input.

tensions.wants: Define child dossier page layout and information architecture
tensions.value: Child dossier page (/department/children/[childId]): tabbed interface. Tabs: Overview (demographic info, current placement status, assigned caseworker), Medical (health data, treatments, disabilities — RBAC restricted), Judicial (court orders, legal status), Schooling (school records, academic tracking), Professional (vocational training, employment), Documents (uploaded files with access log), Reports (activity reports from establishments, chronological list), Messages (discussion thread), History (audit log of all changes to dossier). Each tab is a separate React component (OverviewTab, MedicalTab, etc.). RBAC hides tabs based on user role: external professionals see only tabs relevant to their assignment (e.g., psychologist sees Medical + Messages only).

doubts.about: Tab visibility and content scope for external professionals not yet defined — RBAC rules per role type (psychologist, teacher, magistrate) missing from PM tensions.
Medical tab data entry forms (treatments, diagnoses, medications) not yet designed — depends on data model schema from data architect.

Schooling and Professional tracking UI not specified in PM scope — may be simple text fields or structured forms with school selection, grade tracking, etc. Affects EducationTab and ProfessionalTab component complexity.

tensions.wants: Define user and access rights management UI for department admins
tensions.value: Admin panel at /department/admin: UsersListPage shows all users in department (social workers, establishment users, external professionals) with role badges, status (active/inactive), last login timestamp. Actions: Invite new user (opens InviteUserModal), Edit user role/permissions (opens EditUserModal with role dropdown + permissions checkboxes), Deactivate user (soft delete, preserves audit trail). Invite flow: admin enters email + selects role, system generates invite link, admin copies link or sends via email (email sending via backend, frontend just triggers API). Permissions editor: matrix view (resources × actions) with checkboxes, pre-filled based on role template, admin can customize per user. Role templates (department_worker, establishment_user, etc.) are backend-defined, frontend fetches via /api/roles.

doubts.about: Granular permissions matrix structure (list of resources and actions) not yet provided by backend architect — EditUserModal permissions checkboxes are placeholder pending backend /api/roles endpoint design.

Email sending for invite links — backend may or may not provide email service in V1. If not, admin must manually copy/paste invite link. Affects InviteUserModal UX (copy button vs 'Email sent' confirmation).
External professional invitation workflow differs from internal users (may require additional approval step or different invite expiration) — not yet clarified by PM.

tensions.wants: Flag missing API contract definitions needed for frontend implementation
tensions.value: Frontend development blocked on several backend API endpoint contracts: GET /api/notifications (notification list schema), POST /api/placements/match (request body and response schema including match scoring), GET /api/children/:id (child dossier data structure with all tabs), POST /api/documents (upload flow and response with signed URL), GET /api/roles (role/permissions matrix structure). Without these contracts, frontend components like NotificationBell, MatchedEstablishmentsList, ChildDossierPage, DocumentViewer, EditUserModal cannot be implemented beyond mockups.

doubts.about: Backend architect may not have finalized API contracts yet — typical in discovery phase, but creates implementation blocker for frontend.

tensions.wants: Flag absence of UX architect journey definitions
tensions.value: UX architect has not yet written ux_* tensions defining user journeys, screen flows, and wireframes. Frontend component structure (especially ChildDossierPage tabs, PlacementRequestWizard steps, Admin panel layout) is currently inferred from PM feature list but lacks user research validation. High risk: components may not align with actual user mental models or workflow needs, especially for low digital literacy users (establishment staff, family assistants). Recommend UX architect prioritize journey mapping for: placement request creation flow, child dossier navigation, establishment request response workflow, admin user management.

doubts.about: UX architect role may not have started work yet, or tensions not yet written to field — blocking frontend design validation.

tensions.wants: Define accessibility and internationalization requirements
tensions.value: Accessibility: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance target (public sector requirement in France via RGAA). All interactive elements keyboard navigable, ARIA labels on icons/buttons, color contrast compliance (Tailwind default palette mostly compliant, verify critical UI elements). Forms show clear error messages, field labels always visible. Document viewer must support screen readers (PDF alt text extraction if feasible). Internationalization: French only for V1 (no i18n library needed). DOM-TOM timezone support via date-fns or dayjs with timezone plugin, display all timestamps in user's local timezone (stored as UTC in backend). Future: multi-language support if platform expands beyond France (not in scope, but component text should be externalizable).

doubts.about: RGAA compliance requirement not explicitly confirmed by PM, but highly likely given public sector client and GDPR compliance mandate — assume mandatory until contradicted.
Timezone handling for DOM-TOM not tested — edge cases (e.g., user in Métropole viewing child in Réunion) may cause confusion if not clearly indicated in UI.

tensions.wants: Assess overall coherence of the pipeline output for client presentation
tensions.value: The pipeline is largely coherent with clear discovery gaps identified, but several blocking inconsistencies and unaddressed dependencies prevent full readiness.

doubts.about: Permission matrix design is partial and blocks backend, data, and front implementations
Placement matching criteria are not exhaustive, affecting multiple components

tensions.wants: Evaluate if the designed scope is realistic given budget signals
tensions.value: Budget is not yet defined, making feasibility unknown, but the scope includes complex multi-tenant GDPR-compliant features across 4 specialist areas, suggesting high development cost.
